programme.title: The Key of David

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dr Sloan and Amanda help the FBI investigate a plane crash near the hospital, but just as they think the case is solved, Jesse announces two bodies have gone missing. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mark recalls an incident from 20 years previously that appears to hold the key to solving the case involving cover-ups, radioactive fuel and the death of an airline rep. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A TV executive is murdered and the subsequent death of the chief suspect makes Mark and Steve realise a serial killer is on the loose. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members are determined to prove their worth after they fail a military training exercise ordered by Director Merrick. Meanwhile, random bomb strikes threaten the internet infrastructure around the city, and Scorpion sets up an operation to find the perpetrator. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members go against Cabe's advice and travel to Las Vegas for what they believe will be a simple job helping the owners of a casino. However, Walter is suspected of performing a heist and is arrested, so Scorpion is left to clear his name. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. DiNozzo, McGee and the FBI continue to track political connections to a case, and Bishop sends DiNozzo a souvenir. Meanwhile, Gibbs and Special Agent Pride chase leads in New Orleans after evidence points to a copycat killer. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Pauley Perrette and Scott Bakula

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee's girlfriend Delilah makes a breakthrough in a case involving the alleged death of a US navy lieutentant. However, when her boss orders her to back away, she turns to the NCIS team for help. Meanwhile, McGee talks to DiNozzo about whether he should move forward with his relationship. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team must ascertain whether the murder of a Navy officer, whose body was found on a rural road, was the result of a bar fight or because he knew too much about an attack on a fellow female officer. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and McGee begin a juice detox to purge their bodies of impurities. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the disappearance of photographer Martin Roe, who was due to testify at an army first lieutenant's court-martial after he captured a picture of what appeared to be a soldier killing an unarmed civilian. However, the photograph is leaked, and Roe is later found dead with a needle in his arm

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Director Vance charges DiNozzo with a special assignment to go to France, locate Admiral Kendall's wayward daughter Amanda and bring her home. Arriving in Marseille, Tony immediately heads to the local NCIS office, only to find five dead agents. After eventually tracking down Amanda, the pair embark on a rollercoaster ride to the relative safety of the nearest NCIS office in Spain. Michael Weatherly stars

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members are determined to prove their worth after they fail a military training exercise ordered by Director Merrick. Meanwhile, random bomb strikes threaten the internet infrastructure around the city, and Scorpion sets up an operation to find the perpetrator. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion

However, Walter is suspected of performing a heist and is arrested, so Scorpion is left to clear his name. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Chief petty officer Leland Wiley dies of a heart attack just as he is about to reveal information on elusive arms dealer Agah Bayar. The team discovers the cardiac arrest was caused by someone hacking into a computer monitoring Wiley's pacemaker and increasing his heart rate to 400bpm. Meanwhile, a probationary agent tries to impress Gibbs and win a place on his team. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with Tamer Hassan and Matt L Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Confidential files are leaked by an anonymous hacker, who threatens to release more unless the Navy agrees to renew a contract with a struggling shipbuilding firm. The Navy Secretary suspects the company's owner Philip Wickes is to blame, and asks NCIS to join forces with the PsyOps unit in an elaborate ruse to bring the culprit out of hiding. Meanwhile, Gibbs' colleagues begin to notice his attraction to his opposite number Samantha Ryan. Guest starring Jamie Lee Curtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Vance's brother-in-law Michael Thomas is linked to the murder of a petty officer, and Gibbs is placed under pressure to clear the man's name. Meanwhile, a negative performance review leaves Tony feeling downhearted and he resolves to prove the assessment wrong by being less chatty while on duty. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rocky Carroll

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: Medium
programme.desc: Allison has recurring dreams about her past, and the strange visions interfere with her efforts to prevent a murder. Starring Patricia Arquette and Jake Weber

programme.title: Medium

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: The team tries to make sure the public is protected from the sun as Sydney endures a heatwave, and treats scores of heatstroke sufferers, including an unconscious girl

programme.title: Bondi Rescue

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jesse's quiet fishing trip with his estranged father takes an unexpected turn thanks to revelations about international espionage. Drama, guest starring Patrick Macnee, Robert Vaughn, Phil Morris and Robert Culp

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Randy thwarts Steve's efforts to catch a thief and claims a mime artist mysteriously appeared in her bed - but by the time the Sloans arrive he has gone. Ken Kercheval guest stars

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A motocross rider is killed while making a film at a local track, and Dr Sloan suspects sabotage may account for the air of tension hanging over the set

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter is forced to enlist the help of former troubled team member and genius Mark Collins when a nuclear reactor at a Los Angeles power plant is on the verge of a cataclysmic meltdown. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team members must pass a mandated psychiatric evaluation or face being disbanded after they are accused of destroying a priceless work of art. Meanwhile, Ralph is reluctant to go to his school's Halloween party. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine is found murdered in his garage, and inquiries lead the agents to his former sergeant. However, Gibbs and the team soon find out that their man is under FBI witness protection as he is about to testify against a mobster, giving him a cast-iron alibi. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A serial killer begins posting footage of his crimes online - and as the agents investigate, they realise that each film contains visual clues to the identity of the next victim. In a further bizarre twist, Abby later finds what seems to be a new video on her computer - and the case takes on fresh urgency with the discovery that it is actually a live feed, suggesting the murderer is somewhere on the premises

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bank at Quantico is robbed and a security guard killed, but the team soon discovers that stealing money may not have been the perpetrators' primary objective. Meanwhile, Gibbs is given the task of evaluating an inexperienced new agent assigned by Vance to help investigate the case. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Rey Valentin

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs sends DiNozzo and Ziva to retrieve top-secret information from a secure military base - a mission aimed at catching a mole in the NCIS ranks. Unfortunately, it soon becomes clear that the traitor is still at large - so the building is locked down and all agents are rounded up in a final effort to catch the culprit. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Having identified the mole in their ranks, the agents arrest the traitor's contact Ted Bankston, a former NSA agent working for a kidnapper code-named the Weatherman - who is making millions on the stock markets out of stolen military data. Drama, with Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Walter is forced to enlist the help of former troubled team member and genius Mark Collins when a nuclear reactor at a Los Angeles power plant is on the verge of a cataclysmic meltdown. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam agrees to help an old friend whose nephew has been kidnapped, and although Granger and Hetty forbid him from investigating the case in company time, they soon change their minds when they discover the culprit is Haakim 'the Ambassador' Hamami, a wanted terrorist with connections to the Taliban. LL Cool J, Chris O'Donnell, Linda Hunt and Miguel Ferrer star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Sam's wife Michelle resumes her CIA cover to investigate a hit on a Russian mob boss with possible links to Sidorov and the stolen nukes. Her husband is understandably unhappy about her involvement - and it seems his worries are justified when concerns grow for her safety. LL Cool J and Chris O'Donnell star, with Aunjanue Ellis, Daniela Ruah and Barrett Foa

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The discovery that a murder weapon used in Idaho matches one used in a national security case back in LA brings Sam and Callen into contact with NCIS: Red, a mobile team of agents who live and work together solving crimes across the US - and soon they are signed up as honorary members to help piece together the puzzle. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The LA and Red agents continue their collaborative effort to track down the elusive Robert Spears, with intelligence from captured terrorist Sayed leading them to El Centro in Mexico. Kim Raver (24) and John Corbett (Sex and the City) guest star with Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An innocent drink at a bikini bar takes a turn for the worse when cyanide-laced water claims the lives of an exotic dancer and a navy lieutenant, and leaves a pilot fighting for his life. It's up to the team to determine whether it was an isolated incident - or a practice run for a large-scale poisoning. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star as the agents

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to create a restaurant garden on Italy's Ligurian coast, but it is not long before their working holiday is disrupted by a murder. Determined to solve the crime before returning home, they find themselves with no shortage of suspects - and matters are complicated further by a second death. Guest starring Suzanne Bertish (Rome) and Tristan Gemmill (Casualty), with Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: An archaeologist posing as Rosemary dies mysteriously during a dig for Anglo-Saxon artefacts in the grounds of Lord Engleton's stately home, presenting the duo with a fresh mystery to solve. The gardens are unaccountably blighted by yellowing, forcing the estate to close its gates to the public, and a second death suggests somebody will go to any lengths for a piece of British history. Guest starring Julian Wadham

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are called in to investigate when gardens entered for a village contest are attacked by a disease. Further sinister undercurrents are exposed when reigning champion Donald Westward is found dead and recriminations start to fly as buried rivalries come to the surface. Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu investigate a missing person case at a casino, where gambling is a matter of life or death. Starring Mark Benton and Jo Joyner

One of the victims turns out to be the son of Rebus's cousin, giving Templar the perfect excuse to take him off the case. However, the copper is determined to trace a schoolgirl he is certain holds the key to the mystery. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: An old friend asks the detective to investigate the death of his father, which he is convinced should be treated as murder. Due to his suspension from the police force, Wallander refuses to get involved - but finds himself drawn back to the case when his acquaintance is also found dead. Drama, starring Kenneth Branagh and Tom Hiddleston, with Rupert Graves, Angela Thorne and Vincent Regan

programme.desc: Dr Hugh Knight's plans to leave Whyhope are scuppered when tragedy strikes, and Penny makes a fateful decision about her love life

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Will joins the CID team as a trainee DC and discovers how tough life can be out of uniform. On his first assignment he goes undercover to tackle the case of a developer who is reported missing following threats from anti-capitalist protesters. The man is found, badly beaten, at his burnt-out building site, and it soon becomes clear he had been defrauding clients to raise money to pay off a ruthless protection racketeer

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Arun is incensed when a gang on the Bronte estate sets upon a young couple. He is then completely taken in by one of the local youngsters, who draws him into a trap that strips him of his dignity - and most of his uniform. Smithy sets out to trace the gang by way of Arun's stolen radio, and the crusading PC is shocked to discover who is masterminding the beatings and muggings - and is all the more determined to nail them this time

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: A missing 15-year-old is found unconscious and badly beaten in an alleyway, a sight that touches a nerve with Leon, who is reminded of events buried in his own past. CCTV footage reveals the girl had argued violently with her barman boyfriend on the night of the attack, but he claims this was because he broke off their relationship after discovering she was much younger than she claimed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Elize Du Toit guest stars as a barrister's daughter with a cocaine habit, who is beaten up and pushed out of a car by her supplier when she fails to settle her debts. Terry goes undercover as a cab driver for the company run by the suspected dealer and his first drop turns out to be more hazardous than anticipated

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Poplar readies itself for Christmas 1960 with a festive bus trip to see the Regent Street lights, while a visit from a BBC film crew promises to make it a festive holiday to remember. However, it's a difficult time for grieving mother Iris, bringing back memories of her long-departed baby daughter. At Nonnatus, things are tense as Sister Monica Joan determines to bring the Christmas spirit early - despite Sister Evangelina's disgust. As the community prepares for a televised carol concert, it becomes apparent that Sister Monica Joan has gone missing. Jenny Agutter and Judy Parfitt star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Easter approaches in the spring of 1961, and Patsy helps to deliver a baby that is born with severe deformities. Her husband struggles to cope with his daughter's needs, but the sisters rally round to support the family. Trixie teaches a keep fit class which results in a medical emergency, leaving her determined to teach her patients to understand their own bodies

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Barbara treats a pregnant woman whose dockworker husband is struggling to support her as opportunities for work dry up. She is forced into a difficult decision that leaves Barbara facing the biggest test of her career. Nurse Crane meets a kindred spirit when she learns to speak Spanish, and Sister Evangelina helps a woman with breastfeeding, leading to unforeseen consequences

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Glass Virgin
programme.desc: Feature-length drama from the pen of the popular storyteller. A woman runs away from her wealthy family after she discovers a shocking secret about her past. Nigel Havers, Emily Mortimer and Brendan Coyle star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Valerie's friend Marjory goes into labour while Lucille is on duty, and the new recruit successfully delivers the baby. However, when Marjory's health takes a turn for the worst, her racist mother Mae accuses Lucille of incompetence. The nurses rally round to protect their new colleague, and convince her that she is welcome in the community. Sister Winifred comes up with a plan to get fathers more actively involved in their wives' pregnancies - but finds that very few of the local men are keen on the idea

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth investigates a friend's claim to have witnessed a murder being committed on a passing train, but everyone is initially stumped by the lack of a body. Feature-length drama, starring Joan Hickson, David Horovitch, Joanna David and Jill Meager

programme.title: The Line of Beauty
programme.desc: Drama, adapted from Alan Hollinghurst's 2004 Man Booker Prize-winning novel. Set in the 1980s, the plot focuses on the exploits of a middle-class homosexual man, who goes to live at the house of a newly elected Conservative MP. Dan Stevens and Tim McInnerny star

programme.title: The Line of Beauty
programme.desc: By the summer of 1986, Nick finds himself living out his dreams, safely installed as a member of the Fedden household and a player on the London gay scene. His boyfriend Wani keeps their affair under wraps, desperate to protect his family name, but as Mrs Thatcher guests at Gerald and Rachel's silver wedding party Nick's world begins to crumble. Drama, starring Dan Stevens and Tim McInnerny

programme.title: The Line of Beauty
programme.desc: The year is 1987 and Mrs Thatcher has again been returned to power. Political scandal engulfs the Fedden house and revelations about Nick force a confrontation with family members. Drama adapted by Andrew Davies from Alan Hollinghurst's 2004 Booker Prize-winning novel, starring Dan Stevens, Tim McInnerny, Hayley Atwell, Alex Wyndham and Alice Krige

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sahira reluctantly declines Hanssen's invitation to join him in attending a conference in Stockholm because it clashes with her wedding anniversary, but the arrival of an intriguing patient gives the director of surgery another chance to win her over. Meanwhile, Chrissie's worries about Dan's sexuality become overwhelming as her own nuptials approach, and Chantelle receives disappointing news that causes her to rethink her carefree attitude to life

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Comic Relief special, co-written by Notting Hill and Love Actually creator Richard Curtis. Nathan is unimpressed by the staff's attempts to raise money for Red Nose Day, but a visit by former team member Megan Roach provides the grumpy director with a lesson in camaraderie. Sam hatches a plan when TV star Angus Deayton comes in to be treated, while Cyd rejects Greg's attempt to apologise. Brenda Fricker guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Concluding the two-part story filmed in London and Germany in collaboration with the cast and crew of drama series SOKO: Leipzig. Jack and his team track the kidnap gang to Germany and help arrange a ransom pick-up at Leipzig railway station, but the operation proves to be a set-up leading nowhere. The case takes a violent turn, prompting an unexpected confession from the kidnapped girl's stepfather (Max Herbrechter) and a desperate race to reach the hostages before it is too late

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Bianca discovers the mysterious whereabouts of Ricky and is pleased he isn't having an affair. Phil's new found friend makes a dramatic impact on his life. Peggy gets a call from Sam.

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Sarah and Ted remain at loggerheads over emigrating to Dubai. Bianca and Lenny continue to clash over Rick's speedway plans. Sarah and Lorraine plan a birthday

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Sarah heads to Sydney with George and Anna to see her ill mother, while Elizabeth decides to reveal her darkest secret in the hope that it will save James and Olivia's marriage. Elsewhere, Sir Henry Swanson makes a request to receive a Japanese trade delegation at Ash Park

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan is welcomed back to Darrowby, where neglected animals and tight-fisted farmers cause problems for the vets. Rural drama, starring Christopher Timothy, Robert Hardy and Peter Davison

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: When the body of SAS officer Tom Sunderland is found in a shallow grave, DI Ken Jackson becomes caught up in a messy love triangle involving the decased's wife and his best friend, fellow army officer Sean Brook

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Tracey spreads her wings for the first time since her husband was put behind bars, and invites a handsome footballer round for dinner. Meanwhile, Sharon discovers the joys of literature. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Ted gets even with entertainment manager Jeffrey by threatening to spill the beans about the dog he keeps hidden in his chalet. Holiday-camp comedy, starring Paul Shane and Ruth Madoc

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth learns the Barker-Finches had a local celebrity at their barbecue, and is desperate to outdo them with her own event - an 'outdoors indoors luxury barbecue and finger buffet'. Comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Wesley, making good use of his engineering background, designs a mobile vending machine suitable for the thirsty and foolhardy. However, when he takes his device to the golf course, it causes more embarrassment for a beleaguered Barry. Gordon Wharmby stars

programme.title: The Coroner
programme.desc: The owner of an ice-cream factory is found dead in a walk-in freezer, and while Davey pursues the man's son as prime suspect, Jane finds evidence to suggests his wife is to blame

programme.title: Luther
programme.desc: The detective takes a long-overdue holiday to look after Jenny and help her get back on her feet, only for their domestic plans to be interrupted by Ripley with news of a violent attack at a petrol station. To add to Luther's problems, Baba's heavies Frank and Toby continue to make their blackmail demands, which he knows he must follow for Jenny's sake - until he finds a way to exploit the antagonism between the two men

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Jack and Strickland form an unlikely alliance that sees them break the rules to find out why a British soldier died while taking part in a Ministry of Defence medical trial. With three of the man's colleagues now suffering from psychological, drink or memory problems, the team considers whether he died as a result of the experiment, or if he was a victim of a cover-up by the Army

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: When an Iranian refugee is burnt to death, it initially appears to be a racially motivated murder, but Burke is convinced there is another motive behind the killing. Detective drama, starring Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: The opening of Hugh's new cardiac clinic is upset by life-changing news from his ex-wife, and Charlie's life is thrown into disarray when her father makes a visit

programme.title: The Heart Guy

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: The Pilgrims arrive in Sutri, a medieval town with an even older history, where they visit a magnificent Roman amphitheatre, where Lesley Joseph auditions for 'producer'. Stephen K Amos, Mehreen Baig and Les Dennis visit a church, which was originally Etruscan and pagan before becoming Roman, then Christian and finally Catholic. Later, with Rome on the horizon, they get their first view of the Eternal City from Monte Mario park, heading to St Peter's Square. Then emotions run high as they learn Pope Francis has agreed to meet them in a private audience. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Points of View

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Croatia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening Group D fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This is, of course, a repeat of the famous World Cup semi-final from 2018, when Croatia broke English hearts and defeated Gareth Southgate's men 2-1 after extra time in Moscow. Kieran Trippier's early free-kick put the Three Lions in dreamland, but Ivan Perisic equalised in the second half, before Mario Mandzukic sealed victory 11 minutes from the end of extra time. Southgate will be hoping for a better outcome this time around, with further matches against Czech Republic and the old enemy Scotland still to come in a difficult group. Croatia have reached the quarter-finals of the European Championship twice, most recently in 2008, and their World Cup final appearance will give the players - including talismanic captain Luka Modric - the belief that they can go even further in this tournament. Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand provide analysis, with comm

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the 10 most memorable moments from the last major tournament to be held in England

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to County Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Eric receives word that his son has been badly beaten at Chapel Grove and is currently in hospital. He gives in to the threats, and agrees to help smuggle drugs to spare his son further harm. Mark considers writing to the wife of the man he killed, and finds that his new cellmate is one of his former students - who accuses him of being a bad teacher. However, he may have a chance to put his skills to use by helping another inmate to learn to read

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Croatia in Group D, and Austria v North Macedonia and Netherlands v Ukraine from Group C. The Three Lions have never won this tournament, but with all three of their group matches being held at Wembley - which is also the venue for the semi-finals and final - hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can perform well this summer

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Croatia. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Guy Mowbray and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a bungalow in Tipton, Dion Dublin meets father-and-daughter developers in Burnley and Martin Roberts examines the potential of three flats in Lewisham, south-east London

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair, and finds out about a distinctive style of wood carving. Experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine assist the teams

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people with interesting items to sell, including a doll designed to teach resuscitation, and a collection of dairy artefacts

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Two famous celebrity ex-couples, Love Island's Michael Griffiths and Joanne Chiminodes, and TOWIE's Diags and Fran Parman, answer awkward questions about their failed relationships

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Hampden Park. With commentary from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team builds a den for Cara, who has a sweet tooth and loves to dance

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Libby and Emerson battle it out, with help from Saturday Mash Up's Joe Tasker and comedians Vic Cook, Marek Larwood and Sukh Ojla

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The new sport badge is revealed

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve tracks down the deadliest of all dinosaur defenders, meeting the armoured tank that was Nodosaurus and testing whether an Iguanodon had what it took to beat a T. Rex. Plus, thanks to some mega engineering, he unleashes an Ankylosaurus tail-swinging robot

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: Is mankind alone, or are there aliens out there, either waiting to be discovered, or on their way to find Earth? Professor Brian Cox spends this episode asking such questions, and what he discovers may raise a few eyebrows. He begins by exploring the human race's efforts to find neighbours in outer space, including the launch of two golden discs containing a greeting from Earth in the 1970s; they are still travelling and are now the most distant man-made objects from the planet. Brian also meets members of the Search for Extra-Terrestrial Intelligence, who have been monitoring radio signals for 50 years without success, before discussing the ingredients needed to make an intelligent civilisation with astrophysicist Dr Frank Drake

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Some of the most vulnerable members of the species struggle to survive, including two orphans whose mothers were killed and a young female who is finding it difficult to deal with unfamiliar emotions. The new 'gorilla king' also has trouble keeping those in his care under control. Narrated by Patrick Stewart

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: Mary visits Scone Palace near Perth, where she is invited to help Lady Mansfield prepare for a special dinner with traditional Scottish reels. She also enjoys salmon fishing on the family's private stretch of the Tay, and the regal pastime of Highland deer stalking with William, the future Earl of Mansfield and the family's long-serving gamekeeper

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan round up this year's nest cameras in Norfolk. Iolo Williams heads to the Moray Firth to check on dolphins, and Gillian Burke encounters basking sharks in Northern Ireland

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on memorable trips to the rainforest, with Chris Packham recalling a visit to Pipeline Road in Panama when he saw a staggering array of birds. Steve Backshall recounts his climb of a sheer sandstone mountain in Venezuela, while Gordon Buchanan talks about his four years living in the rainforest while learning his trade as a cameraman

programme.title: Oklahoma! (1955)
programme.desc: A farm girl is torn between two very different suitors - a charming cowboy wandering through the territory and a brooding farmhand with a dark side to his nature. Meanwhile, another cattle driver has finally raised enough money to marry his sweetheart, only to find he has a rival. Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical Western, with Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones and Rod Steiger. Songs include Oh What a Beautiful Mornin' and I Cain't Say No

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie narrates a celebration of cinema's finest musicals, featuring archive BBC interviews with some of the genre's biggest stars and directors from Hollywood's golden age of star like Marilyn Monroe, Jane Russell, Gene Kelly and Fred Astaire to the modern-day triumphs of La La Land and The Greatest Showman. The programme also explores the connection between box office and music charts, and looks at how stars like the Beatles, Elton John and Abba saw their songs cross over to record-breaking sensations of the silver screen

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The search for an exciting new name in British photography continues. This week sees the six contenders challenged to capture the character of flowers in rural Herefordshire, the adrenalin of urban free running and impress Rankin with a brand advertising assignment

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Blackburn, Anita Manning values a gold charm bracelet and Philip Serrell takes a shine to an unusual globe. Paul Martin finds out about the life of singer Kathleen Ferrier

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist looks at attempts to answer one of the most profound questions we can ask - are we alone in the universe? With scientists sending space probes to the furthest reaches of our solar system and beyond, the scientific search for alien life has begun and Brian explains why this quest deserves to be taken seriously, and explores the chances of extraterrestrials being found

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day three of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Huw Edwards presents highlights of this morning's military display from Windsor Castle, where the Colour of the former 2nd Battalion Scots Guards was trooped to mark the Queen's official birthday. With reports by Sonali Shah

programme.title: Phil Collins at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of the singer's performances on BBC programmes from Top of the Pops to Parkinson and The Two Ronnies, as well as the hits that saw him top the charts multiple times here and in the US. Including You Can't Hurry Love, A Groovy Kind of Love, One More Night, I Missed Again and Two Hearts

programme.title: Buster (1988)
programme.desc: Fact-based crime drama about the life of Buster Edwards, a member of the gang responsible for the Great Train Robbery. Going on the run after the notorious heist, he flees to Mexico with his family. However, his homesick wife struggles to adapt to her new life, leaving the outlaw with a difficult choice. Starring Phil Collins, Julie Walters, Sheila Hancock, Larry Lamb and Ralph Brown

programme.title: Kinky Boots (2005)
programme.desc: A man inherits a struggling shoe factory, but salvation arrives when he comes up with a plan for the business to manufacture specialist footwear for drag artists. However, the stunned workforce has to be won over, and the new designs must prove their worth at a high-profile fashion show. Comedy drama, starring Chiwetel Ejiofor, Joel Edgerton, Nick Frost and Linda Bassett

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler shows how to stock up vegetables for the colder months of the year as she grows courgettes, squashes, kale and Jerusalem artichokes. She also learns how to preserve food by using easy pickling recipes, forages for seeds and plants winter crops to keep her going until the next spring

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets volunteers in the market town of Chorley in Lancashire, where major construction work in the town centre is proving a massive challenge for the group

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don turns his attention to containers, planting them up for drama and summer colour in the jewel garden and showing us how to re-pot citrus plants. In Hampshire, Joe Swift meets the designer of an award-winning contemporary garden to explore the techniques and the plants she has used to seamlessly integrate both house and garden

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is on Arran finding out how a tree disease is changing the island's landscape. JJ Chalmers tries his hand at dry stane dyking, and there is a look at how the Royal Highland Show team plan to put on an online spectacular

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson, Brian Cunningham and George Anderson focus on people gardening in smaller spaces. Kirsty plants up a range of trendy containers with plants to attract pollinators, while George is growing quick crops in small spaces

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Boxing
programme.desc: Action from Paris, where Great Britain's leading amateur boxers aimed to secure their spots at the Tokyo Olympics

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin visit a flat with sea views in Margate in Kent and an end terrace in Longbridge in the West Midlands

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Canon Kate Bottley joins pilgrims, including the Bishop of Durham and writer Sheridan Voysey, as they walk one of the new pilgrimage routes to Durham Cathedral

programme.title: Summer Holiday (1963)
programme.desc: Four mechanics embark on a fun-filled trip across Europe on board a double-decker London bus, and enjoy all manner of romantic adventures with three young women who hitch a ride on their way to Athens. However, an unexpected stowaway causes a few surprises. Musical comedy, starring Cliff Richard, Lauri Peters, Melvyn Hayes, Una Stubbs, Ron Moody and the Shadows

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Damion Burrows and Lizzie Fraher compete to remodel Natasha and Gael's home in Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, which has a dark and claustrophobic downstairs layout that makes social gatherings impossible. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson appraise items at the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall, turning up a few gems including an old teapot

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland and Northern Ireland as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn Cassells and Sandra Baer face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie Mitchell's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, hill farmer Johnny Irvine and his son Raymond are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee Budge from Shetland puts her pony skills to the test

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: An exploration of the continent in which Ade examines how African nations may influence international affairs in the next 50 years as life expectancy, population, and economic growth rise. He starts by travelling from Cape Verde to his birthplace in Nigeria, passing through Senegal and Ivory Coast along the way. He discovers how a region once ravaged by the slave trade is still being exploited by Western companies, who dominate the fishing trade, plays a game of para soccer on the streets of Lagos and works with salt collectors at Senegal's Lake Retba

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from day four of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. The sides had not met here in this format since 1999, when Alex Tudor was player of the match as the hosts prevailed by seven wickets

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters look back on trips across the continent, with Gordon Buchanan reflecting on a year in the forests of Minnesota getting to know a family of wild black bears. Chris Packham recalls a magical moment when a sperm whale popped up alongside his boat, as well as the time he got a little too close for comfort to a grey whale's blowhole, while Liz Bonnin recounts the time she filmed alligators in Florida during their breeding season

programme.title: Brian Cox's Adventures in Space and Time
programme.desc: The physicist takes a fresh look at the concept of gravity, revealing it to be far more than just the force that makes things fall to the ground. He revisits his previous programmes, including being first on the scene to meet a capsule returning three astronauts from the International Space Station, and demonstrating how a bowling ball and feather fall at the same speed in the largest vacuum chamber in the world

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The explorer travels to Alaska, the northernmost American state, to discover how three communities have learned to survive amid its wild terrain. He begins by sampling life with a family of salmon fishers, before heading to Nome, a city famed for a gold rush at the start of the 20th century - and discovers its modern-day prospectors often find their best hauls at the bottom of the sea. Finally, he joins the Inupiat people, who have lived in Alaska for thousands of years, and discovers why their annual whale hunt is under threat

programme.title: The Big Sick (2017)
programme.desc: A struggling stand-up comedian proves a hit with a student heckler, but their affair ends just before a mystery illness puts the object of his affections in a coma. Finding himself absorbed with Emily's care and bonding with her sparring parents, Kumail also has to struggle with his own Muslim relatives and their expectations. Romantic comedy, starring Kumail Nanjiani, Zoe Kazan, Holly Hunter and Ray Romano

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Kian discovers that Lucky betrayed him by calling social services, and as plans begin to move Andrei away, a desperate and powerless Kian spirals out of control. Ange and Josh are questioned about their secret relationship by Hanssen, leaving them worrying about their future at Holby, and Dom feels rejected when a patient requests Sacha to be her consultant

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Izzie Balmer explore the antiques emporia of Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire. Phil has his eye on a few quality antique staples, as well as one hefty horticultural item. Meanwhile, Izzie finds herself in what could well be her perfect shop, containing lots and lots of her favourite sparkly pieces. A magnificent Elizabethan Country House provides the backdrop for their final face-off

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Rajeena and Hardeep have moved to their dream house in Great Glen boasting incredible views. However, its garden is an uninspiring blank canvas with an ugly bank of grass that obscures their vista rather than the traditional garden with a modern twist that they would prefer. The bank needs sorting and they want an entertaining area that capitalises on their view across the fields. A water feature and privacy is also important to the pair. Brothers David and Harry Rich compete with Charlie Dimmock to have their designs accepted to transform the patch on a £3,500 budget

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Metal worker Dominic Chinea takes on a former fairground family's water can, while Suzie Fletcher tackles a miniature camel saddle - the only memento from a childhood spent in Malaysia. This curious item had been safely by owner John's side for 60 years, until an unfortunate incident with a mischievous puppy left it in tatters. Teddy bear repair duo Amanda and Julie welcome a 100-year-old RAF mascot bear called Bobby

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Tennis: Queen's
programme.desc: The cinch Championships. Sue Barker presents coverage of day one of the men's grass-court tournament at Queen's Club, featuring matches from the first round. This tournament, along with Wimbledon, was cancelled last year due to the Covid-19 pandemic, meaning the players will be all the more eager to impress and adapt to their grass-court surroundings, and the seeded players include stars such as Matteo Berrettini, Diego Schwartzman, Milos Raonic and Stan Wawrinka

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Anita Rani, Chris Ramsey, Janet Ellis and Jamie Theakston test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling on to the capital city of Madrid, Michael visits the 1930s art deco Telefonica Building and discovers what happened there during the civil war

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: England v New Zealand. Action from the fifth and final day of the series-concluding Second Test at Edgbaston. England's last Test at this venue was in 2019, when they lost to Australia by 251 runs, denying them a fifth straight victory here and ending a eight-match unbeaten run

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Will Foster compete to remodel a couple's 1950s home in Belfast, with Lei Ying dreaming of a perfect kitchen, while her husband David hopes for a cosy home and a safe route to the garden. Angela Scanlon presents

programme.title: Peter Taylor: Ireland After Partition
programme.desc: In a personal journey to mark the centenary of the partition of Ireland in 1921, award-winning journalist and documentary-maker Peter Taylor revisits the films he has made over the past 50 years to reflect on the issue of a united Ireland. He reveals how his own understanding of it has evolved, from the early days when he took a Hull bus driver and his wife to Belfast, and a group of Catholic and Protestant children to Wales, through to his interviews with MI6 officers and others involved in the secret talks between the British government and the IRA

programme.title: Inside No 9
programme.desc: Dawn and Mick are hosting their annual Last Night of the Proms party as the perfect antidote to post-Brexit Britain, with everyone expected to wave their Union Jacks and sing along to Land of Hope and Glory, But Dawn's sister Penny has seen a mysterious stranger lurking around the neighbourhood and her husband Brian isn't feeling particularly patriotic and by the time the fat lady sings everyone's lives will be changed for ever. Darkly comic drama, written by and starring Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith, with Sarah Parish and Julian Glover

programme.title: Festival of Funny: Shorts
programme.desc: Single mother and immigrant Sonia works in sheltered accommodation, so when a resident makes an official complaint against her, she must continue to face everyone without bias. Comedy, starring Maria Popistasu

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Just Call Me Martina
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents a profile of former tennis star Martina Navratilova, documenting her achievements on the court in a professional career that spanned more than 30 years and saw her win Grand Slam titles in four different decades. Martina also goes back to her home town of Revnice in the Czech Republic, and allows the cameras to film her wedding in New York and the celebrations in Miami. Featuring contributions by Chris Ever, Elton John, Stephen Fry, Billie Jean King, Novak Djokovic, Rafael Nadal, Pam Shriver and Rod Laver

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Norfolk/Suffolk border, visiting a social enterprise harnessing the healing power of the rural landscape and providing training in traditional skills to homeless people. Charlotte also visits local cheesemakers, and meets a woman who gave up a career in logistics to fish for lobsters. Plus, Adam Henson looks at how biobanks could secure the future of rare breeds

programme.title: The Black American Fight for Freedom
programme.desc: Fifty years on from the promise of equality and the Civil Rights Act, this documentary examines moments where America had the chance to become more equal and why that didn't happen. Through interviews with those who were at the forefront of the struggle for a fair system in education, housing and criminal justice, the film reveals the key court cases, Supreme Court rulings and laws that failed to bridge the gap

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: As the third lockdown begins to lift in the middle of April 2021, emergency cases inevitably begin to rise, which puts an incredible amount of pressure of bed availability and staff across the country. At Coventry's University Hospital, Critical Care is at full capacity, and two neurosurgeons must find a solution to treat both of their patients with only one bed available

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A Year in the Wild
programme.desc: Exploring 12 months in the lives of people and wildlife inhabiting the New Forest National Park in Hampshire. Although only created in 2005, its woodlands are among the oldest in the country and have supported lifestyles dating back to medieval times. Residents including coppicer Dave Dibden show how their traditions have continued into the present day, and the film also features footage of some of Britain's rarest creatures, including sand lizards, Dartford warblers and fellow birds hobbies. Narrated by Hermione Norris

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller journeys through Poland from the port of Gdansk in the north to the Slovakian border in the south, visiting Auschwitz, Czestochowa and Warsaw. He explores the historical heritage of the nation, finds time to perform a cameo role with Poland's most famous cabaret group and appears on breakfast TV

programme.title: The Young Montalbano
programme.desc: All preparations for Salvo's move to Genoa have been made, but the inspector himself does not yet seem ready to leave his beloved Sicily. When a young woman is found dead at the bottom of a cliff, having apparently been involved in a tragic car accident, Montelbano is drawn into one last investigation. Augello, Salvo's eager replacement, is quick to accept an easy solution, but his boss remains convinced the inconsistencies need to be examined. Crime drama, starring Michele Riondino. In Italian

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Documentary examining the use of physically imposed discipline, and how attitudes toward its application have changed over the years. The film investigates the ways schools, religion and the justice system have been involved with corporal punishment, and how its imposition has also provoked debate from a sexual aspect

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Former detective Sarah Lund is recalled from her low-key job in the countryside by her ex-boss at Copenhagen police HQ after a lawyer is found murdered in macabre and puzzling circumstances. Meanwhile, newly appointed Minister for Justice Thomas Buch oversees intricate negotiations between parliamentary parties concerning the introduction of new anti-terrorism laws. Starring Sofie Grabol and Nicolas Bro. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund investigates the possibility of a link between Islamic fundamentalists and the murder of lawyer Anne Dragsholm, which causes Buch problems as he tries to push his anti-terror legislation through parliament. Starring Sofie Grabol and Nicolas Bro. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Raben escapes from prison and sets out in search of a former army comrade. Police arrest their chief suspect, an Islamist activist, but Lund clashes with Strange over the direction of the investigation. Meanwhile, a newly discovered memo puts Buch on the defensive. Crime thriller, starring Sofie Grabol, Ken Vedsegaard and Mikael Birkkjaer. In Danish

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The veteran traveller journeys through Poland from the port of Gdansk in the north to the Slovakian border in the south, visiting Auschwitz, Czestochowa and Warsaw. He explores the historical heritage of the nation, finds time to perform a cameo role with Poland's most famous cabaret group and appears on breakfast TV

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of composer Vaughan Williams' The Lark Ascending, which was written in 1914 as Europe approached the First World War. The programme culminates in a performance by 15-year-old violinist Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews at Shirehampton Public Hall, Bristol, where the piece was premiered in December 1920. Featuring contributions by actor Peter Sallis, performer Tasmin Little, and critic and friend of the composer, Michael Kennedy

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Opera singers from around the world perform at St David's Hall in the prestigious international competition. In the opening round, singers from Mongolia, Venezuela, China and Georgia compete for a place in the grand final, performing music by music by Verdi, Rossini, Donizetti and Mozart and accompanied by the Welsh National Opera Orchestra conducted by Michael Christie. This year's judges are Welsh baritone Neal Davies, American soprano Roberta Alexander, and Aidan Lang, general director of Welsh National Opera

programme.title: The Search for the Lost Manuscript: Julian of Norwich
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez tells the story of the first book written in English by a woman, Revelations of Divine Love by Julian of Norwich. The book was written in 1373 and presents an alternative viewpoint on man's relationship with God, which led to it being suppressed for more than 500 years. She travels from Norwich to Cambrai in Northern France, revealing how the book survived and the stories of women who championed it, and consults with academics and theologians about the book's importance

programme.title: The Sky at Night
programme.desc: Chris Lintott and the team look at companies spearheading the boom in Britain's space industry. Plus, Pete Lawrence discusses ice crystals in the upper atmosphere lit by the sun

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the cultural history of Kenya, revealing how artists, musicians and writers have responded to its colonial past and asserted a new identity

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the achievements and discoveries of the Cassini space probe mission, which changed the way scientists regard the solar system. Its 13 years spent orbiting Saturn brought revelations of gigantic ice geysers, hidden underground oceans that could harbour life and a brand new moon coalescing in the planet's rings. Narrated by Paterson Joseph

programme.title: Treasures of the Louvre
programme.desc: Andrew Hussey traces the history of the art museum in Paris, and investigates what its collection of masterpieces has to say about France and the country's rich culture. The programme examines the changing face of the 800-year-old building, which started off life as a fortress, and explores how its most famous exhibit - Leonardo da Vinci's Mona Lisa - came to be stolen in 1911. It also reflects on the Louvre's recently opened Islamic Gallery

programme.title: The Lark Ascending
programme.desc: Diana Rigg explores the enduring popularity of composer Vaughan Williams' The Lark Ascending, which was written in 1914 as Europe approached the First World War. The programme culminates in a performance by 15-year-old violinist Julia Hwang and pianist Charles Matthews at Shirehampton Public Hall, Bristol, where the piece was premiered in December 1920. Featuring contributions by actor Peter Sallis, performer Tasmin Little, and critic and friend of the composer, Michael Kennedy

programme.title: The Search for the Lost Manuscript: Julian of Norwich
programme.desc: Historian Dr Janina Ramirez tells the story of the first book written in English by a woman, Revelations of Divine Love by Julian of Norwich. The book was written in 1373 and presents an alternative viewpoint on man's relationship with God, which led to it being suppressed for more than 500 years. She travels from Norwich to Cambrai in Northern France, revealing how the book survived and the stories of women who championed it, and consults with academics and theologians about the book's importance

programme.title: Return to Pembrokeshire Farm
programme.desc: Work on the derelict miller's cottage appears to be progressing, but Griff Rhys Jones meets opposition from the locals over his plans to renovate the mill. Plus, the comedian takes on another unusual restoration project

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints a winter scene with a touch of warmth and golden sunlight

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and expert Philip Mould explore the history of three paintings bequeathed to the National Museum of Wales when their owner died in 1951. It was always believed the pieces were by landscape artist JMW Turner, but only months after the museum took ownership, experts branded them fakes and unfit to hang on the gallery walls. Now Philip sets out to prove their authenticity once and for all

programme.title: Great British Photography Challenge
programme.desc: The photographers face three final assignments, including a wildcard challenge where they are given free rein to try and get one last extraordinary shot for their portfolios. They are also in Glencoe for a dramatic landscape challenge and must curate and mount what they hope will be a winning exhibition for the judges. Choosing a maximum of 15 images from the hundreds they've taken across the course of the series is a huge task in itself

programme.title: BBC Cardiff Singer of the World
programme.desc: Petroc Trelawny presents coverage of the second round of the contest from St David's Hall, with singers from Denmark, South Korea, South Africa and the home nation of Wales competing for a place in the final. Performing music by Mozart, Verdi, Mendelssohn and Handel, the entrants will be accompanied by the BBC National Orchestra of Wales, conducted by Andrew Litton

programme.title: Britain in Focus: A Photographic History
programme.desc: In this series, Eamonn McCabe traces the story of British photography, from the rapid innovation of Roger Fenton in the 1800s to the satirical eye of Martin Parr in the 21st century. In the third and final episode, he focuses on the explosion of colour images in the late 1950s, to the ongoing impact of the digital revolution

programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out about the exciting things people do to train to become an astronaut. Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. She finds out how they learn how to move in space when they can’t keep their feet on the ground! We discover there is less gravity in space than on Earth and although astronauts look like they are floating, they are free falling.

Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a night light, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that could be used as part of a bedtime routine. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate bedtime objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The baby lullaby Little One Time For Bed features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show what they’ve been doing at bedtime too. The Baby Bear Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

Ashley meets Bobbles who takes the role of old Father William to do some impressive back-flips and balance an eel on the end of his nose.
