programme.desc: Babies and their grown-ups play with hairbrushes and enjoy songs and story time together

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: On a lovely day, Duggee is watering the plants while the squirrels play nearby. Happy goes to retrieve the ball that is stopped by Mole, who is sitting on a bench looking sad

programme.title: School of Roars
programme.desc: Wufflebump creates some chaos when he accidentally brings his monster dog Growlbert to Show and Tell. The naughty dog buries all the items and Miss Grizzlesniff is forced to send him to the squishy corner. However, when a door handle catastrophe takes place, an unlikely hero saves the day

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Stompy Chomper runs around hungrily eating rocks. Can B.O.T. succeed in this mission?

programme.title: Andy's Baby Animals
programme.desc: Talking is important for young animals. Andy Day sees how lions aren't born with a loud roar. In Antarctica a penguin chick can be heard by his mum despite 150,000 others. A baby macaque must learn the value of funny faces if she is to fit in

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Let's Play
programme.desc: Sid steps through the magic curtain to be a rock musician and play in a concert, but must find the missing singer before the show starts

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: When a spaceship lands, the Olobobs have trouble communicating with their visiting friend. With some laughter and the pulling of funny faces, they're soon off on a space ride

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch goes on a desert adventure to uncover a lost city, but is disappointed to learn someone else has found it already

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 95 million years in search of an eggshell belonging to one of the biggest animals to ever walk the Earth

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle makes two new doggy friends leaving Evie feeling a bit left out until Waffle reassures her. The two new dogs escape into Mrs Hobbs' garden, so Waffle has to save the day

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Sam and Ernie are building a beach hut, Jack offers to fetch a feather duster to help clean it and it reminds him of an adventure he once had with a giant

programme.title: Woolly & Tig
programme.desc: Woolly reminds Tig how important her Aunt Shonagh's wedding is as the duo do their best to ensure the day is fun for everyone

programme.title: Yakka Dee!
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say ‘chicken’, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure! We see lots of chickens – a brown chicken, a white chicken, a clucking chicken and some friendly chickens. Dee meets a funky chicken, a hatching chicken and a cock-a-doodle-do-ing chicken! The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee’s friend finally succeeds in saying today’s word!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Introducing the long 'U' sounds, including the worlds blue, glue and rescue

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug escape a very noisy bookshop to find Granny Pug, who is leading a mediation retreat. They are trying to find a peaceful spot - sometimes it works and sometimes it doesn't. They return to the bookshop where Dog can make some noise

programme.title: Messy Goes to OKIDO
programme.desc: When Messy finds a dusty sock underneath his bed, he goes to Okido to find out why it makes him teary

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour green, from grass to grapes. We explore the meaning of being green, and the episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour green.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin spends the day with a radio star at her school. They record a rap for CBeebies Radio. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble and Grandad Tumble run Tumble FM Radio Show

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po run around in circles in Home Dome. The Noo-noo joins in and the Teletubbies have fun chasing

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Flop is feeling poorly, so Bing decides to look after him by making him comfy on the sofa, giving him a blanket and reading him a Hoppity Voosh story

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Greedy Grandmaster Glitch has been gorging on chocolate and wants more. The Grimbots discover a place called Chocolate Hills on Bohol Island in the Philippines and take him there

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The sheep and his friends discover their playground has been covered by a layer of frost, but while everyone else has fun sliding around, Kid struggles to keep his balance

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: A chill strikes at Pupton Park, and leaves the Footy Pups snowed in. Meanwhile, Rachel teaches her team the importance of warming up before a match

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that go off-road

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain's precious whistle gets blocked up and stops working, so Cook and Line try to replace it with other silly whistles

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: When the Squirrels find Duggee playing saxophone, they want to have a go too

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When the auditions for Mr Jeremy Fisher's annual recital attract the attention of Mr Tod, it's up to Peter to save the day with some musical mischief of his own

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Firefighter Karen shows us her fabulous helmet. We see how important it is for Karen to wear it while she does her heroic job, as well as some of the amazing things it does.

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day attempts to catch the breed of dragonfly that a baby Allosaurus hunts and eats, and watches as more hatch from their eggs and take their first steps - before facing a race against time to make it back to the museum

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: It's bedtime and Maddie is finding out about different things we sleep on and in. At a campsite she uses a special thermal camera to show how the layers in a sleeping bag work by trapping warm air inside to keep us warm

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: In a game of Work, Bluey and Bingo are the latest hires at Dad's drainpipe factory.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The four superheroes take part in a disco-dazzling around the world game show

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: When a tarantula visits the vet, Molly helps Mack overcome his fear of big hairy spiders

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle feels left out of the Brooklyn-Bells' fun, so he sets off to find his doggy family

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo & Gran Gran go on a day trip to the seaside to build sandcastles!

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: When Bread Man needs help, Sarah and Duck come to the rescue with their tandem bike

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion looks inside a book and decides to tell a story to his best friend Dibillo

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: A Tin Bird lands on the Clangers' little blue planet

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos hear Igglepiggle's musical stepping stones and Upsy Daisy's singing

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Angellica Bell reads No-bot: The Robot with No Bottom, by Sue Hendra

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Tish and Anna Haugh and special guest Emma Bunton. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Live coverage of the pageantry and spectacle of the ceremony to mark the official birthday of Queen Elizabeth II, performed by British and Commonwealth army regiments

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers hop on their motorbikes for a culinary voyage of discovery along the Namibian coastline. They set up a traditional barbecue known as a braai in the Germanic town of Swakopmund, finding the best spot to cook among the dunes by taking to the air in a microlight

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television, here discovering the truth about football in Wales. It's a story of weekend warriors, boggy pitches and dodgy tackles, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Wales: The Big Kick Off
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Nathan Blake present this curtain-raiser to the Euro 2020 tournament, ahead of the national side's first game against Switzerland. They are joined in the studio by current and former Wales internationals to reminisce about the heroics of 2016 and get their take on this competition, and check in on the squad from their training camp in Cardiff. Plus, the Alarm perform the official Euro 2020 single, The Red Wall of Cymru

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: Elis James' Feast of Football
programme.desc: Regular guest Danny Gabbidon joins the presents in Cardiff for reaction to Wales' opening match of Euro 2020 against Switzerland

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic legend Sarah Storey, former England cricketer Ryan Sidebottom, world champion swimmer Mark Foster and European gold medal-winning sprinter Adam Gemili join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland. Another chance to see the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the topical magazine show covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: The Pilgrims arrive in Sutri, a medieval town with an even older history, where they visit a magnificent Roman amphitheatre, where Lesley Joseph auditions for 'producer'. Stephen K Amos, Mehreen Baig and Les Dennis visit a church, which was originally Etruscan and pagan before becoming Roman, then Christian and finally Catholic. Later, with Rome on the horizon, they get their first view of the Eternal City from Monte Mario park, heading to St Peter's Square. Then emotions run high as they learn Pope Francis has agreed to meet them in a private audience. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Croatia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening Group D fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This is, of course, a repeat of the famous World Cup semi-final from 2018, when Croatia broke English hearts and defeated Gareth Southgate's men 2-1 after extra time in Moscow. Kieran Trippier's early free-kick put the Three Lions in dreamland, but Ivan Perisic equalised in the second half, before Mario Mandzukic sealed victory 11 minutes from the end of extra time. Southgate will be hoping for a better outcome this time around, with further matches against Czech Republic and the old enemy Scotland still to come in a difficult group. Croatia have reached the quarter-finals of the European Championship twice, most recently in 2008, and their World Cup final appearance will give the players - including talismanic captain Luka Modric - the belief that they can go even further in this tournament. Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand provide analysis, with comm

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the 10 most memorable moments from the last major tournament to be held in England

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to County Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce heads to the waterfront beside the V&A Dundee design museum, on the east coast of Scotland. She explores the fascinating story behind the landmark museum and the treasures it houses. Among the discoveries are a valuable bowl found at a car boot sale for just £3, a set of rare SAS medals and a Vivienne Westwood jacket

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Eric receives word that his son has been badly beaten at Chapel Grove and is currently in hospital. He gives in to the threats, and agrees to help smuggle drugs to spare his son further harm. Mark considers writing to the wife of the man he killed, and finds that his new cellmate is one of his former students - who accuses him of being a bad teacher. However, he may have a chance to put his skills to use by helping another inmate to learn to read

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Croatia in Group D, and Austria v North Macedonia and Netherlands v Ukraine from Group C. The Three Lions have never won this tournament, but with all three of their group matches being held at Wembley - which is also the venue for the semi-finals and final - hopes are high Gareth Southgate's side can perform well this summer

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: England v Croatia. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Wembley Stadium. With commentary from Guy Mowbray and Jermaine Jenas

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell revisits a bungalow in Tipton, Dion Dublin meets father-and-daughter developers in Burnley and Martin Roberts examines the potential of three flats in Lewisham, south-east London

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville return to examine more disastrous holidays and travel stories, beginning by hearing from passengers whose dream cruise was ruined by a fire on board - which meant missing out the highlight of the trip

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: A scribbled name in a discarded notebook is the key to putting a dangerous driver behind bars, while the serial numbers on stolen cash cracks the mystery of a dangerous gang

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from Ardingly antiques fair, and finds out about a distinctive style of wood carving. Experts Philip Serrell and Roo Irvine assist the teams

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people with interesting items to sell, including a doll designed to teach resuscitation, and a collection of dairy artefacts

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Elis James: Football Nation
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian looks at how a turbulent period for Welsh football turned into one of international triumph. A long line of mangers had endured mixed results since the early Nineties. However, the investment in youth initiated by John Toshack and the good work of Gary Speed before his tragic death helped to lay the foundation for qualification for Euro 2016 and 2020

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Hampden Park. With commentary from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin examine a two-bedroom flat in Godalming, a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford, and a terraced property in Smethwick

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help contestants seek out items of value at the East of England Arena in Peterborough to sell for profit at auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp shows off an interesting find with a family connection

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland. Another chance to see the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan

programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher hosts the topical magazine show covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Lynsey Brennan of Dundonald Parish Church and Rev William Boyle of Port Glasgow New Parish Church lead Reflections, considering the call of God. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers are in Luton, where they meet Elizabeth and Benjamin Lovett. They and their children are a fun-loving family but their garden is a drab, which is a shame as they practically live in it. The outdoor space is very special to them as they have numerous special needs which make going out very challenging. Whose design will the Lovetts love most?

programme.title: Points of View

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond is on Arran finding out how a tree disease is changing the island's landscape. JJ Chalmers tries his hand at dry stane dyking, and there is a look at how the Royal Highland Show team plan to put on an online spectacular

programme.title: Paddington 2 (2017)
programme.desc: The lovable little bear has settled into his new life with the Brown family in London, becoming a much-loved member of the local community - until his search for a birthday present for his aunt leads to him being framed as a thief. While the Browns try to clear Paddington's name, he tries to make himself at home in prison, winning over the other convicts and getting involved in an escape attempt. Comedy adventure, starring Hugh Bonneville, Hugh Grant and Sally Hawkins, with Ben Whishaw providing the voice of Paddington

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani head to County Down to catch up with the dairy farming Lilburn family. Elsewhere, Adam Henson discovers how science is helping to save rare breeds, Charlotte Smith investigates a shadow pandemic of rural domestic violence, and John Craven launches the 30th Countryfile Photographic Competition

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: Judges Anna Campbell-Jones, Michael Angus and Kate Spiers convene at House for an Art Lover by renowned Scottish architect Charles Rennie Macintosh to decide which of the nine regional finalists will win this year's competition

programme.title: Time
programme.desc: Eric receives word that his son has been badly beaten at Chapel Grove and is currently in hospital. He gives in to the threats, and agrees to help smuggle drugs to spare his son further harm. Mark considers writing to the wife of the man he killed, and finds that his new cellmate is one of his former students - who accuses him of being a bad teacher. However, he may have a chance to put his skills to use by helping another inmate to learn to read

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents action from the day's three games - England v Croatia in Group D, and Austria v North Macedonia and Netherlands v Ukraine from Group C. programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic (Kick-off 2.00pm). Eilidh Barbour presents coverage of the Group D fixture at Hampden Park, as the hosts play their first game at a major tournament since 1998. Steve Clarke's side ended almost 23 years of pain back in November, when they overcame Serbia in a memorable penalty shootout in Belgrade. Their most recent European Championship appearance was even further back, at Euro 96, but they failed to make it past the group stage by the tightest of margins - Patrick Kluivert's consolation goal for the Netherlands against England meant the Dutch made it through by virtue of goals scored. The Czechs have qualified for every European Championship since Euro 96, when they made it all the way to the final. They failed to win a game in France back in 2016, however, so they will be hoping for a better showing this time around. Commentary comes from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers welcomes four more people with interesting items to sell, including a doll designed to teach resuscitation, and a collection of dairy artefacts

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the stories that matter from across the country

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Spain v Sweden (Kick-off 8.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of both sides' opening game in Group E, which comes from Estadio La Cartuja in Seville. Three-time champions Spain will enter this tournament as one of the favourites to go all the way, given their rich history and world-class players at their disposal, although their recent displays in major finals have left a lot to be desired. Barcelona veteran Sergio Busquets was due to lead his country this time around, in the absence of Spain's all-time record appearance holder Sergio Ramos, but the midfielder tested positive for Covid just over a week ago, throwing manager Luis Enrique's plans into chaos. Sweden haven't made it past the group stage at the European Championship since 2004, and finished bottom of their pool at Euro 2016, failing to win a single game. Alan Shearer, Rio Ferdinand and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Steve Bower and Danny Murphy

programme.title: Do Black Lives Still Matter?
programme.desc: Grime artist Saskilla examines companies' pledges to address racial inequalities after the Black Lives Matter protests, speaking to insiders from a number of household brands before, during and after the initial protests following George Floyd's death. He also talks to black influencers, who found themselves temporarily becoming flavour of the month to larger businesses

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Two famous celebrity ex-couples, Love Island's Michael Griffiths and Joanne Chiminodes, and TOWIE's Diags and Fran Parman, answer awkward questions about their failed relationships

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race Down Under
programme.desc: The remaining contestants take part in a talent show to win a place in next week's grand final and get one stage closer to becoming the show's first Drag Race Superstar from Australia or New Zealand. Featuring a special appearance from pop duo the Veronicas

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Scotland v Czech Republic. Another chance to see the opening Group D fixture for both sides, which took place at Hampden Park. With commentary from Rob MacLean and James McFadden

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Ben Tish and Anna Haugh and special guest Emma Bunton. Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Live coverage of the pageantry and spectacle of the ceremony to mark the official birthday of Queen Elizabeth II, performed by British and Commonwealth army regiments

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin examine a two-bedroom flat in Godalming, a house in a terrible state of repair in Telford, and a terraced property in Smethwick

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe help contestants seek out items of value at the East of England Arena in Peterborough to sell for profit at auction. Plus, presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp shows off an interesting find with a family connection

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. Wales are entering their second consecutive European Championship, having never previously qualified before their memorable debut appearance in 2016. They emerged top of a group also containing England, Slovakia and Russia, and defeated Northern Ireland in the last 16 before an unforgettable victory over heavy favourites Belgium in the quarter-finals. Chris Coleman's team eventually succumbed to Cristiano Ronaldo and Portugal in the semi-finals, but their remarkable run to the last four will live long in the memory for their fans. Switzerland have qualified for their fifth European Championship, and will be looking to make it to the knockout rounds for the second consecutive time. They pushed Poland hard in the last 16 in 2016, but eventually lost 5-4 on penalties. Arsenal's Granit Xhaka was the only player to miss

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Denmark v Finland (Kick-off 5.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening game of Group B, which takes place at Parken, Copenhagen. The Danes famously won this trophy back in 1992, but have only reached the knockout rounds once since then. They failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and finished third in their group at the 2012 tournament in Poland and Ukraine, despite beating the Netherlands in their opening match. Finland are making their debut at the European Championship, having finished second behind Italy in their qualifying group. Their talismanic striker Teemu Pukki will be hoping to make an impact on the biggest stage, having starred for Norwich City since his arrival in 2018. Micah Richards, Alex Scott and Cesc Fabregas provide analysis, with commentary from Jonathan Pearce and Martin Keown

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present a celebrity edition of the music-based quiz show, with comedian Susan Calman, former footballers John Barnes and Chris Kamara, and DJs Vick Hope, Melvin Odoom and Scott Mills. The pressure's on to name as many hit songs and artists as possible to win £10,000 for their chosen charities

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A couple from Manchester answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Dr Christian Jessen, Dr Ranj Singh, Matt Richardson, Andrew Maxwell, Katie Thistleton, Michelle Ackerley, Mark Moraghan and Tina Hobley take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie is brought into the ED once again with mysterious symptoms, and Rash becomes suspicious of Rosa's behaviour, leaving him convinced she is deliberately poisoning Ollie. Ethan once again pushes for Fenisha to spend time with Matthew instead of himself, and a construction worker brings her baby to the site, and leaves her in a shed that is about to be demolished

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the day's three games - Wales v Switzerland in Group A, and Denmark v Finland and Belgium v Russia in Group B. Wales won their most recent match against the Swiss in 2011, and were hoping to make a winning start to their campaign in Baku. The Russians will have been out for revenge against a star-studded Belgian side, who beat them twice in qualifying

programme.title: Thierry Henry: My France, My Euros
programme.desc: The former France striker and Arsenal's all-time leading scorer looks at European Championship stories, assessing the social and cultural impact football has across the continent. First shown as Henry's home country prepared to host Euro 2016 following November 2015's terror attacks in Paris, he explores how the beautiful game can unify a nation, from the success of the Netherlands in 1988 to Euro 96 in England, when 'football came home'. Contributors include Cesc Fabregas, Peter Schmeichel, Ruud Gullit, Jurgen Klinsmann, Alan Shearer, David Baddiel and Ian Broudie

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: Wales v Switzerland. Another chance to see the opening Group A fixture for both sides, which took place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Rome
programme.desc: The Pilgrims arrive in Sutri, a medieval town with an even older history, where they visit a magnificent Roman amphitheatre, where Lesley Joseph auditions for 'producer'. Stephen K Amos, Mehreen Baig and Les Dennis visit a church, which was originally Etruscan and pagan before becoming Roman, then Christian and finally Catholic. Later, with Rome on the horizon, they get their first view of the Eternal City from Monte Mario park, heading to St Peter's Square. Then emotions run high as they learn Pope Francis has agreed to meet them in a private audience. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: England v Croatia (Kick-off 2.00pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the opening Group D fixture, which takes place at Wembley Stadium. This is, of course, a repeat of the famous World Cup semi-final from 2018, when Croatia broke English hearts and defeated Gareth Southgate's men 2-1 after extra time in Moscow. Kieran Trippier's early free-kick put the Three Lions in dreamland, but Ivan Perisic equalised in the second half, before Mario Mandzukic sealed victory 11 minutes from the end of extra time. Southgate will be hoping for a better outcome this time around, with further matches against Czech Republic and the old enemy Scotland still to come in a difficult group. Croatia have reached the quarter-finals of the European Championship twice, most recently in 2008, and their World Cup final appearance will give the players - including talismanic captain Luka Modric - the belief that they can go even further in this tournament. Alan Shearer and Rio Ferdinand provide analysis, with comm

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: President Biden is in the middle of his first overseas tour, taking in the G7, the EU, NATO and a meeting with Vladimir Putin. America, he says, is back - another way of telling the world the Trump era is well and truly over. programme.title: Slighean Sgoile an t-Saoghail (Most Dangerous Ways to School)
programme.desc: Lake Titicaca, straddling the border between Peru and Bolivia in the Andes Mountains, is one of South America's largest lakes. Living here are the Uro - an ancient people, described at times as the only decedents of the Incas. They are the people of the floating islands, a very small community of about 2,000 with a community consisting of 50 swimming islands. Every day from the furthest corners, the youngest inhabitants paddle their way in their small boats to school

programme.title: Celtic Connections
programme.desc: A Canadian trio perform one of their tunes mixing traditional-folk with a trance-folk beat

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Hillwalker Colin MacLeod travels to Perthshire, where he climbs Ben Lawers, which is situated between Loch Tay and Glen Lyon. He also explores the history behind the name given to the Schiehallion mountain, the place where 18th-century astronomer Nevil Maskelyne first worked out Earth's density

programme.title: Sgoil nan Sagart/Priest School

programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township, where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. This film celebrates a way of life which has positively shaped this dynamic landscape and explores the challenges that the crofters face

programme.desc: Freaturing acclaimed singer Robert Robertson, Irish supergroup Altan and vocalist Siobhan Wilson. Plus, Ruth Keggan from the Isle of Man and Flavia Coelho from Brazil. Hosted by Cathy MacDonald

programme.title: Crowdie and Cream
programme.desc: Three-part series based on Finlay J MacDonald's well-known trilogy about childhood, family, home and friendship. programme.title: Kerry is Kirsty
programme.desc: Cycling through Assynt on the North West coast, sisters Kerry and Kirsty MacPhee go off-road to follow an old peat track at Clachtoll before continuing along the coast to Stoer lighthouse. They meet up with Ruairidh MacKay, who has fond memories of coming to the area when he was a young boy

programme.title: Speaking Our Language
programme.desc: An opportunity to learn Gaelic. Presented by Rhoda MacDonald

programme.title: Earrann Eachdraidh (History Shorts)

programme.title: An Lot (The Croft)
programme.desc: Donald Macsween is back with a special series from his croft in Ness. Cameras follow him from the beginning of the year and into lockdown, getting his thoughts on how the coronavirus has impacted him in his job as a full-time crofter

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: The 1950s and '60s saw many young women from the islands in Scotland head to the mainland to work in hotels all over the country. With little work on offer at home, for many there was no choice, while others followed older girls to an exciting, new life. This documentary hears the stories of some of those 'hotel girls'

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Karen Matheson performs versions of Calum Sgaire and Cànan nan Gàidheal, with an international cast of musicians, and with Galician musicians Xabier Diaz and Guadi Galego

programme.title: Bha mi Latha Samhriadh (When Stornoway Was Dancing)
programme.desc: A warm-hearted look back at aspects of the social life of Stornoway following the Second World War. It was a time of enormous change, with increasing wealth and a new culture imposing itself through music and film, but for a few halcyon years the baby-boomers uniquely enjoyed the best of both worlds

programme.title: Seòid a' Chidsin - The Kitchen Coves
programme.desc: Uisdean and Roddy cook with alcohol, preparing beercan chicken, mojito cheesecake and hake in a spicy batter, accompanied by tartar sauce and a hot salad

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Belgium v Russia (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of both sides' opening Group B match, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. Roberto Martinez's Belgium enter this tournament as one of the favourites for glory, with star names including Eden Hazard, Kevin De Bruyne, Romelu Lukaku and Thibaut Courtois among their ranks. However, their squad is statistically the oldest at the tournament, and this could be the final opportunity for their so-called 'golden generation' to claim a major trophy. Russia reached the semi-finals back in 2008, but have not made it past the group stage since then, and finished bottom of a pool also containing Wales, England and Slovakia at Euro 2016 in France. programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson's quest for the best pizza in Rome leads them to the Bugg Brothers and their latest culinary caper.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: After many hours playing his favourite video games at the arcade, Jay's got some mad skills, so Nya and Jay take a dance lesson together. Next, something is going on in Ninjago City - the infamous Mechanic is off on a crime spree with his henchmen. What is the Mechanic up to, and who is this mysterious Unagami? Finally, everyone in Ninjago City is talking about a new craze - the arcade game Prime Empire. Popular reporter Gayle Gossip critically examines the darker side of this popular trend.

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: It's only three days until the next Speedway Five-Billion race and the stakes have never been higher - the winner takes home the coveted Key-tana. Next, Jay finds himself lost inside the game Prime Empire, but how did he get here? Hunted by the menacing Red Visors, he hides in the trunk of a car, only to wind up in a death-defying chase... Finally, Jay discovers that there's a lot more to Prime Empire than just fun and games - his rescuer Scott helps him hide, but just who is Scott anyway?

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Celebrity chat, music and comedy, presented by father-son duo Martin and Roman Kemp, who are joined by guests Gaynor Faye, Shayne Ward, Jools Holland and Vic Reeves

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Actress and Julie Hesmondhalgh joins Alan at Manor Farm to talk about her BBC One drama The Pact, while carpenter Wayne Perrey creates his very own garden pizzeria. Plus, a report on a couple of show-stopping chickens

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Llama, Miss Monster, White Tiger, Kangaroo and Turtle perform again for guest judge Leah Remini and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Group B hits the stage with Frog, Elephant, Kitty, Taco, Mouse and Banana trying to impress Jenny McCarthy, Robin Thicke, Nicole Scherzinger and Ken Jeong. Nick Cannon presents.

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous (2005)
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie Hart is taken off active duty and made the face of the bureau's publicity campaign, but would rather catch crooks than be the focus of media attention - so when her friends are kidnapped, she disobeys orders to run her own undercover investigation with the help of a short-tempered colleague. Comedy sequel, starring Sandra Bullock, Regina King, William Shatner and Heather Burns

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Austria v North Macedonia (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the opening fixture in Group C, which takes place at National Arena Bucharest. North Macedonia are making their debut at a major tournament, having qualified via the play-offs with victories over Kosovo and Georgia. Captain Goran Pandev, who holds the record for most appearances and goals, will lead out his country, but the Genoa striker and his team-mates face a tough test here against Austria, who were in the same qualification group and won both games between the sides. The Netherlands and Ukraine are the other nations involved in a group that looks to be wide open. Ashley Cole, Emma Hayes and Ian Wright provide match analysis, with commentary from Joe Speight and John Hartson

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Netherlands v Ukraine (Kick-off 8.00pm). All the action from the Group C match, which takes place at Johan Cruijff ArenA in Amsterdam. The Dutch are historically one of the strongest countries in this tournament, winning the trophy in 1988 and reaching the semi-finals on four other occasions. However, they failed to qualify for Euro 2016 in France, and didn't make it out of their group in 2012. Ukraine are appearing in their third consecutive European Championship, and qualified with an undefeated record, finishing above Portugal and conceding only four goals in eight games, with Andriy Shevchenko at the helm. These countries have, perhaps surprisingly, only faced each other twice, with both matches friendlies. The most recent was a 1-1 draw back in August 2010, with Jeremain Lens and Olexandr Aliyev getting on the scoresheet in Donetsk. Mark Pougatch is joined by Roy Keane, Gary Neville and former Dutch international Nigel De Jong, with commentary from Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: Billion Pound Bond Street
programme.desc: Bond Street has been London's most exclusive shopping street for 300 years. The glamorous half mile between Oxford Street and Piccadilly boasts more luxury brands and more royal warrants than any other in Britain. In this documentary narrated by Jenna Coleman, cameras take a look at how this lucrative region has survived during the pandemic

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: Promoted to Detective Chief Superintendent, Bancroft has professional success but at great personal cost. When a double murder brings an unexpected reunion, Elizabeth is forced onto the sidelines by Detective Superintendent Jake Harper. However, as new enemies emerge, old foes make their move. Thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery, Charlotte Hope, Adam Long and Lee Boardman

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Loose Women

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team head to Lincoln, where Fay Rutter appraises some Beatles memorabilia, Tony Geering dithers over an oil lamp and Helen Gardiner sends a Rolex watch to auction - where it is well received

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking three contestants to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals
programme.desc: More of the most thrilling and nerve-racking endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Poland v Slovakia (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening fixture in Group E, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. Poland only made their debut at the European Championship in 2008, but they have appeared in both subsequent tournaments and reached the quarter-finals of Euro 2016 in France, losing in a penalty shootout to eventual champions Portugal, and with Robert Lewandowski still leading the line, they will be confident of providing a stern test for anyone. Slovakia are playing in their second consecutive European Championship and qualified via the play-offs, defeating Republic of Ireland and then Northern Ireland to reach this stage. Their captain - Napoli legend Marek Hamsik - is now almost 34, and may see this as his final opportunity to perform on the biggest stage. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with Eni Aluko, Nigel de Jong and Ally McCoist, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: During the latest trip to the ever troubled region of the Dales, Aaron finds Liv, Bernice is determined and Meena covers her tracks with David

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Seema Jaswal introduces the key moments from the Group D match between Scotland and Czech Republic at Hampden Park. Emma Hayes and Scott Brown provide the expert analysis. Plus, updates from the home nations as England and Wales both reflect on their first matches. With commentary by Sam Matterface, Seb Hutchinson and Tom Gayle

programme.title: Bancroft
programme.desc: As the murder evidence begins to build, Harper and the team are met with conflicting accounts. Meanwhile, Elizabeth finds herself taunted by one of the suspects and the trouble around the Kamara deal, forcing her to choose between her personal and professional loyalties. As the chief superintendent is deserted by her allies, she enters a dangerous game of cat and mouse. Crime thriller, starring Sarah Parish, David Avery and Charlotte Hope

programme.title: Tipping Point: Best Ever Finals

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Poland v Slovakia (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the opening fixture in Group E, which takes place at Saint Petersburg Stadium. Poland only made their debut at the European Championship in 2008, but they have appeared in both subsequent tournaments and reached the quarter-finals of Euro 2016 in France, losing in a penalty shootout to eventual champions Portugal, and with Robert Lewandowski still leading the line, they will be confident of providing a stern test for anyone. Slovakia are playing in their second consecutive European Championship and qualified via the play-offs, defeating Republic of Ireland and then Northern Ireland to reach this stage. Their captain - Napoli legend Marek Hamsik - is now almost 34, and may see this as his final opportunity to perform on the biggest stage. Presented by Mark Pougatch, with Eni Aluko, Nigel de Jong and Ally McCoist, and commentary by Sam Matterface and Lee Dixon

programme.title: Emmerdale

programme.title: Tomb Raider (2018)
programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

programme.desc: After her father's disappearance, kickboxing heiress Lara Croft makes money as a bike courier and refuses to claim her inheritance, convinced her dad is still alive. But when she learns the country estate could be sold off unless she claims it, she reluctantly agrees - and finds a pre-recorded message from her father giving clues to his possible whereabouts. Determined to find him, she sets out across the sea to Hong Kong - but soon crosses paths with a small army of mercenaries determined to break into a supposedly haunted tomb. Action adventure based on the video game, starring Alicia Vikander, Dominic West, Walton Goggins and Kristin Scott Thomas

But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. programme.desc: The sleuth falls victim to depression after failing to prevent the murder of a society girl by notorious art thief Marrascaud. His confidence shattered, Poirot eventually returns to work when a lonely chauffeur begs him to find his missing soulmate, the maid of a famous Russian dancer. The investigation takes him to the Swiss Alps, where, against all odds, he stumbles upon a hotel thought to be Marrascaud's hideout - leading to a very personal showdown. The penultimate episode of the long-running detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Simon Callow, Orla Brady, Patrick Ryecart, Nigel Lindsay, Morven Christie, Tom Austen and Rupert Evans

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: First episode of the costume drama set in an Edwardian country house in 1912, written by Oscar-winning writer Julian Fellowes. The lives of the Crawley family and Downton Abbey's servants are changed for ever when the sinking of the Titanic leaves the estate without its heir and his son. The arrival of a new valet shocks the staff, who scheme to make his stay as short-lived as possible. Starring Maggie Smith, Hugh Bonneville and Elizabeth McGovern

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: When an RAF officer is found hanged in a wood, Meredith - the new chief superintendent at Hastings - is quick to dismiss it as a straightforward suicide. However, an aerial photo of a German village found in the dead man's pocket leads Milner to suspect murder. The newly retired Foyle is compelled to return to service when events take an even more unexpected turn. Wartime crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: In 1838, at the age of 18, young princess Alexandrina's life is transformed when she ascends the throne of Great Britain and becomes Queen Victoria. As monarch in her own right, the young Victoria suddenly finds herself freed of the repressive shackles placed on her by her mother's advisor, Conroy, and builds a new alliance with the charismatic prime minister Lord Melbourne. However, her inexperience makes her susceptible to mistakes - and ultimately scandal. Period drama, starring Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Victoria
programme.desc: As Lord Melbourne's popularity in the house wanes, the Tories eagerly await their imminent triumph, while the Duke of Cumberland views it as a chance to prey on his niece's vulnerability and establish a co-regency with the Duchess of Kent and Conroy. Victoria is devastated when Melbourne regretfully announces he is stepping down and even more bewildered that she must ask complete stranger Sir Robert Peel to form a government, and she desperately implores Melbourne to return. Period drama, starring Jenna Colman and Rufus Sewell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A young woman asks Poirot to investigate her father's murder - a 14-year-old crime for which her mother was found guilty and executed. The detective agrees to investigate, but warns his client that he may discover things she does not want to hear. David Suchet stars, with Rachael Stirling, Aidan Gillen, Aimee Mullens, Toby Stephens, Gemma Jones, Patrick Malahide and Annette Badland

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective finds himself investigating the strange and gruesome murder of an elderly psychiatrist. programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 15th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the men's singles final in the Grand Slam tournament. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple

programme.desc: Coverage of the 14th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the women's singles final in the Grand Slam tournament. Also scheduled to take place today is the final of the men's doubles competition. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.desc: A series of damning reports claims police officers are not playing by the book, so Regan poses as an ex-con and heads to a club frequented by two officers suspected of running a protection racket and taking bribes from villains to release them. John Thaw stars

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Suffering from nervous exhaustion, Holmes sets off for a break on the wild Cornish peninsula, where he hopes the peace and quiet will help him convalesce. Unfortunately, several murders ensure his holiday is far from relaxing. Period detective drama, guest starring Denis Quilley

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: The brother of the late Clement Armstrong devises a plan to avenge his death at the hands of the cybernauts by setting a deadly trap for Steed and Emma. Peter Cushing guest stars

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An amnesiac woman delivers a garbled message about an attack on a diplomatic meeting planned for the following day. She later regains her memory and tells Bodie and Doyle her employers were abducted by the same gunmen who intend to carry out the action. Gordon Jackson, Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur excludes Terry from his latest business venture, prompting him to embark on a search for alternative employment. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: A youth is arrested after an elderly money-lender is brutally assaulted and robbed. His mother asks Regan, an old friend, to intervene. Though it is later proved he got a legitimate loan and is innocent of theft, the youngster finds himself being blackmailed after falling foul of some very dangerous individuals. Karl Howman guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Coronation Street's Hayley Tamaddon, football star Geoff Hurst, comedian Rob Beckett and presenter Gaby Roslin pit their wits against ruthless quiz geniuses in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: Cameras focus on life at Leeds Bradford airport during the summer of 2018. It may not be the UK's biggest, but what it lacks in size, it makes up for in ambition. With owners having green-lit a multi-million pound investment, the airport is undergoing a huge makeover. There are also problems for the staff due to an emergency landing after a plane's cockpit warning alarm shows its door is still open. Meanwhile, terminal manager Nathan has to deal with a blocked international arrivals door. Hugh Dennis narrates

programme.title: Yorkshire Airport
programme.desc: The airline auditors pay a surprise visit to Leeds Bradford airport, which leaves baggage boys Jimmy and Michael in fear for their jobs when they manage to lose a piece of luggage. Duty manager Nathan has to deal with a flood in the toilets next to an executive departure lounge, and new recruit Gabby faces a trial by fire at the Departure Gate. She has to tell the members of an irate family that they will not be sitting together on their flight to Palma, and struggles to control the ensuing confrontation

programme.title: Gordon, Gino and Fred: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Having previously toured their own countries, Gordon Ramsay, Gino D'Acampo and Fred Sirieix now hit the highways of of the States - although in the first episode, they are in Mexico, on a mission to taste the world's best breakfast. Along the way, the trio take part in a chili-eating competition, have a beach buggy race and watch some traditional Mexican wrestling - but it isn't long before they are persuaded to take part

programme.title: Jack Reacher (2012)
programme.desc: A former army sniper is arrested following a random shooting that leaves five people dead, but protests his innocence. Action thriller based on Lee Child's series of novels, starring Tom Cruise, Rosamund Pike, Werner Herzog and Robert Duvall

programme.title: French Open Tennis: Roland-Garros Live
programme.desc: Coverage of the 14th day's play at Roland-Garros, featuring the women's singles final in the Grand Slam tournament. Iwa Swiatek prevailed last year, comfortably overcoming Sofia Kenin 6-4, 6-1 to become the lowest-ranked French Open champion in the history of the WTA rankings. Also scheduled to take place today is the final of the men's doubles competition. John Inverdale presents, with analysis from Jim Courier, Fabrice Santoro, Marion Bartoli, Mark Petchey, Anne Keothavong and Sam Smith, commentary by Nick Mullins and Jonathan Overend, and reports from Celina Hinchcliffe

programme.title: River Monsters

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: Architect George Clarke delves into the world of small builds, meeting people taking tiny, unpromising spaces and creating places to live, work and play, demonstrating how big dreams can be achieved in small and affordable places. In the third episode, George meets a couple crafting a magical den for their toddler with a helping hand from a grandparent, while an aircraft technician turns a jet engine's housing into a unique camper. Elsewhere in Chile, George discovers some lip-smacking designs in a winery

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Emily and Kris in the village of Somersham, Cambridgeshire, who are unsure whether to sell their three-bed semi or renovate instead. Primary school teacher Emily thinks they need a bigger home with more space for their growing children, but Kris feels their home has too much potential and would prefer to stay put

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: The series concludes with a look at the lives of Prince William and Prince Harry, and the future of the crown.

programme.title: Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secret childhood of Queen Victoria's eldest grandson, the future Kaiser Wilhelm II, through a long-hidden cache of intimate family letters. Wilhelm was born with a permanently paralysed arm - a disability considered shameful at the time - and was subjected to a series of bizarre and often cruel attempts to cure the condition. These childhood experiences are believed to have helped create a highly dysfunctional connection between the boy and his mother, Victoria, Princess Royal, and to have shaped his love-hate relationship with her country

programme.title: Grace Kelly: Lost Tapes of a Princess
programme.desc: Documentary following the life of the movie star turned princess, featuring previously unseen home movie footage from the archives of the Principality of Monaco. These films, shot away from the public eye, capture her home life and her relationship with her children. The film also features contributions from friends and family, including Prince Albert of Monaco

programme.title: Jason Bourne (2016)
programme.desc: Former government operative Jason Bourne has been in hiding for 12 years after exposing a sinister CIA operation. However, he once again finds himself being hunted down when a former CIA operative gone rogue acquires top secret information - including details of Bourne's own father's involvement in a clandestine scheme. Action thriller, starring Matt Damon, Tommy Lee Jones, Alicia Vikander and Julia Stiles

programme.title: Ghost in the Shell (2017)
programme.desc: A cybernetically enhanced soldier designed to battle the world's deadliest criminals faces a new threat - a terrorist who has developed the ability to hack into people's minds and control them.
