programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray's efforts to take an interest in one of Ally's hobbies backfire, leaving him searching for a particularly rare trading card to make amends. American comedy, starring Ray Romano, Madylin Sweeten and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Arthur Williams and Baroness Tanni Grey-Thompson present review of the event from Leeds, plus the AJ Bell World Paratriathlon Series event

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Stephen Mangan talks about his children's book. Konnie Huq chats about the BFI's See Yourself on Screen Challenge. Jessie Ware discusses her latest projects and performs in the studio. Scott Mills talks about his new podcast, Pupdates. YouTuber and musician Yung Filly discusses the YouTube Originals film, Raise Your Game With Gareth Southgate. Adam Duritz chats about the new Counting Crows album, Butter Miracle, Suite One

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer realises he knows very little about Lisa and tries to fill the gaps in his knowledge by hiring a dodgy private detective to spy on her - only to end up on the wrong side of the law. With the guest voice of Elliott Gould

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer takes Ned Flanders' advice and starts praying regularly, hoping God will lend a sympathetic ear and make it possible for him to buy a better home. Marge warns that his materialistic attitude may incur divine retribution, but he turns a deaf ear - until a flood of biblical proportions hits Springfield

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Scarlette Douglas is in Paphos in Cyprus with couple Pauline and Andy. After getting engaged on the island, the pair would now like to find their own bolthole here to share with their family for extended breaks. With the thought of one day also retiring out here, the pressure is on Scarlette to show them five top properties within their budget of £80,000

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix explores Majorca's coast in a sailing boat, and meets a family who are attempting to launch a restaurant on the north of the island

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: Cameras follow people who live and work in the national parks, beginning with the residents of Bleak Bank Farm in the Dales

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales and the Lakes
programme.desc: Craft mason and mountain rescuer Pete Roe is called out to try and find a puppy that has fallen down a 50-foot sinkhole near Wensleydale

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Coast vs Country
programme.desc: Expats Andrew and Anouchka live in Sweden but are planning to move back to east Scotland. Kerr Drummond and Kirsty Duffy shown them various options by the coast and inland

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge's to-do list is as long as ever, and with no guests, weddings or events at the chateau this year, they use their engineering and design skills to tackle some major projects. First on the list is transforming one of the old outbuildings into a supersized workshop for chateau-sized jobs

programme.title: Britain's Most Luxurious Hotels
programme.desc: Exclusive access to Chewton Glen, a five-star country house hotel in Hampshire, where preparations for an extravagant outdoor Halloween party are in full swing - at least until the weather takes a turn for the worse. Managing director Andrew receives some bad news - the opening of his new high-end venture, The Mayfair Townhouse, has been delayed due to the Covid-19 pandemic, while some celebrity guests struggle with an uncooperative champagne cork

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: The seven remaining contenders spend 48 hours undergoing the most psychologically gruelling stage of the training, designed to test their ability to resist interrogation. The recruits are hooded and exposed to endless white noise, and face brutal tactical questioning from a specialist team, while those who endure this experience face an even greater challenge on the highest point on the island

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces critiques shows including The Masked Dancer, Dirty Dancing, Best of Top Gear, Naked Attraction, Tom Allen's Quizness, Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back, and The Bad Skin Clinic. Participants include Nick Grimshaw, Denise van Outen, Mo Gilligan, Shaun Ryder, Bez, Martin and Roman Kemp, Gyles Brandreth, Maureen Lipman, Iain Stirling, Laura Whitmore, Lorraine Kelly, Tom Jones, Anne-Marie, Jamie Carragher and Clara Amfo

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Singers Claire and Louise arrive at the restaurant to provide a bit of moral support to each other, making a foursome with Mathew, who works in a supermarket, and engineering manager Scott. But there's confusion over who will be dating who. Meanwhile, 22-year-old Aryeh hopes fellow student Jack will not be put off by his overwhelming personality and 60-year-old Meryl from Wirral is looking for someone to keep up with her when she goes clubbing. Will Whitchurch divorcee Andrew be up for a dance?

programme.title: Fargo
programme.desc: Deafy's suspicions about Detective Weff's shady affiliations are confirmed, Gaetano pays the piper, Odis receives an unexpected ultimatum and Oraetta's sunny disposition cracks as she loses control. Meanwhile, Josto challenges orders from New York, Rabbi puts his life on the line and Loy attempts to gain an advantage

programme.title: Benjamin (2018)
programme.desc: An up-and-coming filmmaker tries to balance a romance with a visiting French music student and the release of his second movie. A lot of time has elapsed since his acclaimed debut and Benjamin is concerned that his follow-up will be a disaster. Writer/director Simon Amstell's comedy drama, starring Colin Morgan, Jack Rowan and Anna Chancellor, with cameos from Mark Kermode and Simon Mayo

programme.title: The Anti-Vax Conspiracy
programme.desc: An investigation into the international anti-vaccination movement and its message that Covid-19 is a myth and part of a government plot to inject people with dangerous vaccines. The documentary goes inside the world of the 'anti-vaxxers', ranging from those profiting from lethal fake cures to Piers Corbyn, brother of the former Labour leader, who has compared the vaccine rollout to experiments in wartime concentration camps

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Essex, partners Mike and Heather hope to impress their guests with a menu of Mediterranean-inspired dishes, but Fussy eaters Jess and Salv can't hide their true feelings about the starter

programme.title: Kirstie's Celebrity Craft Masters
programme.desc: Broadcaster Natasha Kaplinsky and TV personality Jo Wood go head to head in Kirstie Allsopp's challenges, such as pyrography-decorated chopping boards

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The coach pulls into Sines in Portugal, where the tourists learn reflexology and then hit the beach for some fun and games

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt aims to solve the age-old mystery of how to smoothly extract ketchup from a bottle - by running across a pond of custard

programme.title: Countdown

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam finally asks Diane to marry him, but she rejects his proposal - making him so angry that he chases her out of the bar and ends up in court charged with assault and battery. Ted Danson and Shelley Long star

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam and Diane decide to get married, but have very different ideas over what sort of engagement ring to buy. Comedy, starring Ted Danson and Shelley Long

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra finally prepares a palatable meal, much to the surprise of her long-suffering family, who have grown accustomed to a steady stream of culinary disasters

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra decide it is high time Marie and Frank had a social circle of their own, and encourage them to make new friends - but the plan backfires. Comedy, starring Ray Romano

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra shocks Ray by revealing she would like more time alone, making him fear their marriage may be heading for the rocks. Comedy, starring Ray Romano and Patricia Heaton

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne falls out with Sherry and flees the apartment after the two have a blazing row. With nowhere else to go, she asks if she can spend the night at Niles's flat, leaving him quaking with excitement. Comedy, starring David Hyde Pierce and Jane Leeves

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Niles receives divorce papers from Maris - and to show he is not as spineless as people think, he signs them immediately. Later, though, he begins to regret his impulsiveness. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer and David Hyde Pierce

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: After Niles asks him whether he should stay with Maris, Frasier wanders the streets in film-noir fashion to find an answer for his long-suffering brother. Comedy, starring Kelsey Grammer

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie aims to restore an old chateau carriage for her mother Isabelle's wedding, while it is moving-in day for Debbie and Nigel as they embark on a new life in France

programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.desc: The return of the programme that provides an insight into the world of high-end pawnbroking. In Sheffield, London Road owner Dan Hatfield and his sister Cherise are trying to seal the deal on some expensive luxury luggage, as well as a collection of rare 18th-century intaglios and a football shirt signed by former England player Paul Gascoigne. At Nikolas Patrick in London, brothers Charlie and Patrick Devine deliver a shock when valuing a luxury watch

programme.title: Steph's Packed Lunch
programme.desc: Steph McGovern presents the weekday magazine, including celebrity guests, food ideas, real-life features and entertainment, lifestyle and consumer stories. Plus, a sideways look at the news in One O'Clock Views

programme.desc: Jasmine Harman is on the Greek island of Crete, showing five properties to childhood sweethearts Catherine and Patrick from Leeds. They've been married for over 20 years and cannot wait to enjoy extended holidays in Crete in a property which can also accommodate their two teenage sons. Jasmine shows them five properties for their £150,000 budget

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Sussex, the first hosts are childhood sweethearts Robin and Maria. Robin is the brains behind the cooking, but it's up to Maria to ensure the hosting is spot on. Sasha and Leslie are next, and Sasha soon reveals her apparent royal 'connection'. On the last night, Karl and Michael think they are in with a chance of nabbing the £1,000 prize, so long as they can keep their bickering at bay

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Trio of scary short stories. Homer is left battling against killer mutants in the ruins of a devastated Springfield. An accident with a matter-transportation device causes Bart to switch bodies with a housefly, and in colonial times Marge is accused of witchcraft

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Liberty makes a shocking discovery. Meanwhile, a jealous Grace confronts Martine after she flirts with Felix, and Juliet gets the wrong end of the stick

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt reports from Argentina on the price of sunflower seeds. Amanda Byram is in Yorkshire's rhubarb triangle, where she investigates how forced rhubarb can grow in complete darkness. Plus, Andi Oliver reveals how much added sugar goes into takeaway milkshakes

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Prue Leith and Dr Rupy Aujla help two busy doctors from Manchester with three children, coming up with a menu that will please everyone and reduce waste. Recipes include a simple chicken and cauliflower traybake, rice pudding that makes use of leftover rice, and a hack that turns an old bag of salad into a mouth-watering salsa verde

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 76-year-old man sustains a head injury after falling down some stairs, and his daughter reflects on how his caring for her mother through her dementia has left him exhausted. A five-year-old girl is brought into the paediatric department with a large splinter lodged in her bottom, and a patient has a lump on her shoulder that has developed since having a fall five weeks ago

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Documentary going on the frontline with police in South Yorkshire, revealing what happens when people decide to take revenge on each other. On a Saturday night in Sheffield, police respond when a man reports that he's being attacked by his partner after she found him with a prostitute. A woman is under siege from a neighbour who claims he is sorting out neighbourhood problems by smashing cars with a hammer. And on the outskirts of Doncaster, there's a brawl outside a supermarket as three men attack someone they suspect of causing their family harm

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics help a drunk driver who has crashed on a duel carriageway but find themselves on the receiving end of a vicious attack. In Bromsgrove, a paramedic's ambulance comes under siege as they try to attend to another drunk member of the public

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, J Willy's Bar and Grille in South Bend, Indiana, which has amassed debts of more than $1million. On finding an inexperienced chef serving food from both the freezer and tins, together with service that reduces one customer to tears, Gordon Ramsay orders an overnight deep-clean of the premises and designs a menu to inspire the kitchen staff

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me

Whose soiree will secure the cash prize of £1,000?

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces

programme.desc: Joey is mistaken for the owner of a Porsche and uses the mix-up to enhance his image. Rachel forces Ross to annul their marriage, and Phoebe tries her best to look after the triplets. American comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Jennifer Aniston

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler celebrate their last night as flatmates, and Ross claims he is too busy babysitting to help Monica as she frantically tries to arrange Rachel's packing before the removal firm arrives. Comedy, starring Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: An Australian dancer (guest star Elle Macpherson) moves in with Joey and he struggles to refrain from flirting. Chandler tries to impress Monica by cleaning their flat, and Rachel is embarrassed by Phoebe's jogging style. Comedy, with Matt LeBlanc and Matthew Perry

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe claims she kissed Ralph Lauren at Rachel's workplace, and room-mate Janine encourages Joey to get in touch with his feminine side, much to Chandler's annoyance. Ross bleaches his teeth in an effort to impress a date. Comedy, guest starring Elle Macpherson

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica's parents arrive for Thanksgiving, leading to trouble for Chandler, who discovers they don't like him because they think he uses drugs. Fortunately, their disapproval soon focuses on Ross instead. Comedy, guest starring Elliott Gould and Christina Pickles

programme.title: Kew Gardens: A Year in Bloom
programme.desc: Nestled in London's suburbs is the Kew Gardens, 320 acres of the most diverse, exciting and important plant life in the world. Cameras follow 12 months in the lives of its staff, beginning in winter. In the Palm House, Solene and her team are making sure the banana plants don't smash through the ceiling

programme.title: Kew Gardens: A Year in Bloom
programme.desc: As the team at Kew Gardens heads into spring. Solene is beginning preparations for the giant waterlily display, which sees the small glasshouse transform from empty to bursting point. In the Rock Garden, supervisor Tom discusses the work of a botanical horticulturist

programme.title: Kew Gardens: A Year in Bloom
programme.desc: It's summer and Kew Gardens is enduring a drought, which means the staff have plenty of watering to do, along with the regular weeding and maintenance. Work continues in the Tropical Nursery too as Sam tends to the orchid collection and Lara busies herself pollinating plants

programme.title: Kew Gardens: A Year in Bloom
programme.desc: The year is drawing to a close as a glorious summer gives way to autumn and Kew Gardens embraces its new colour palette. On Cherry Walk, 6,000 tulip bulbs are being planted for spring flowering and Tony encourages the team before visiting the arboretum to investigate why some of his trees are showing signs of seasonal shock. In the kitchen garden, Helena is planting onions in the autumn in a bid to avoid an onion-munching bug. She shows off some potato substitutes and pumpkins, while also selling produce to raise seed money

programme.title: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The royal family have always tried to project an immaculate image, but this documentary reveals what goes on behind closed doors. Stories from the household's staff include a cook revealing the Queen's surprisingly simple favourite treat and a footman of the Queen Mother recounting his most embarrassing gaffe, involving wine and Princess Margaret. Also, historian Kate Williams examines some of the most bizarre and shocking servant duties from ancient times

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm

programme.title: Our Yorkshire Farm
programme.desc: As the nine Owen children finish school for Easter, they have other lessons to learn on their parents' hill farm. Edith and Violet learn about the highs and lows of the lambing season, while Clemmie comes to the aid of a lamb with a broken leg. Reuben and Miles teach Sidney how to build a dog kennel for their runaway terrier

programme.title: Princess Anne: The 7 Loves of Her Life
programme.desc: A look at the romantic relationships of the Queen's only daughter, from early partner and Olympic equestrian gold-medallist Richard Meade up to Timothy Laurence, her husband of 28 years. The programme asks who these men were, but also how the notoriously private Anne emerged relatively unscathed while her siblings and their partners found themselves embroiled in tabloid gossip and scandal

programme.title: The Bodyguard (1992)

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: On a 15-mile walk in North Yorkshire, Kate sets off from the picturesque settlement of Robin Hood's Bay, where archaeologist Maria explains how, in the 18th century, the fishing village's position made it an idea drop-off point for contraband shipped from Holland and France. From here, Kate heads south along the coast, meeting up with geologist Will, who reveals what dinosaur footprints can reveal about the creatures that once roamed the area. After arriving at her destination in Scarborough, Kate dons her wetsuit to join a group of swimmers braving the North Sea

programme.title: The Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at Whitakers Chocolates in Skipton, North Yorkshire, as family-run firm dating back five generations, and the programme following Ellie Wharrad as she hand-paints fashion designer Paul Smith's autumn collection onto chocolate pumpkins. Plus, a look at chocolate adverts from the past including Frank Muir's Fruit and Nut campaign and the 'Finger of Fudge' jingle during a delve into the history of Cadbury's bars

programme.title: Paw Patrol

programme.title: FernGully: The Last Rainforest (1992)
programme.desc: A fairy living in a forest encounters a member of a lumber crew and magically shrinks him down to her size. He learns about the damage humans are doing to the area, and helps defend it when his fellow loggers unwittingly unleash an evil spirit that had been entombed in a tree. Animated adventure, with the voices of Robin Williams, Christian Slater and Tim Curry

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price

programme.title: Monster Trucks (2016)
programme.desc: A drilling operation unleashes a subterranean creature which feeds on oil. The monster bonds with a monster truck which a local teenager is building from spare parts, and the youngster befriends the beast and helps it to return to its underground home. Fantasy adventure, starring Lucas Till and Jane Levy

programme.title: Megamind (2010)
programme.desc: An evil genius finally succeeds in killing the superhero who has foiled his every evil scheme, but finds life without an enemy boring. To fill the gap, he transforms an ordinary man into a worthy adversary - but when his creation turns evil, it falls to the villain to save the world. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Ferrell, Tina Fey and Brad Pitt

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: Evolution (2001)
programme.desc: Two science teachers investigate a mysterious meteor and discover it is oozing a strange fluid containing millions of minuscule but rapidly evolving organisms - which start to turn into terrifying alien monsters with a taste for human flesh. Sci-fi comedy, starring David Duchovny, Orlando Jones, Julianne Moore, Seann William Scott, Dan Aykroyd and Ted Levine

programme.title: The Greatest Showman (2017)
programme.desc: After losing his job as a bank clerk, PT Barnum creates and develops his circus in New York in the mid-1800s with the help of playwright Phillip Carlyle and an adopted family of entertainers shunned by society. Musical drama, starring Hugh Jackman, Zac Efron, Michelle Williams, Rebecca Ferguson and Zendaya

programme.title: Film4 Interview Programmes
programme.desc: Stars and directors talk about the experience of making movies in this compendium of personal and practical reflections

programme.title: X-Men Origins: Wolverine (2009)
programme.desc: Years before he joins the superhero team, mutant Logan is a member of a top secret military unit, but quits because he cannot justify his squad's brutal methods. However, when the woman he loves is murdered by his psychotic brother, Logan returns to his old enemies for help in seeking revenge - and is offered the chance to become indestructible. Superhero adventure prequel, starring Hugh Jackman and Liev Schreiber

programme.title: Raw (2016)
programme.desc: After a trainee vet and strict vegetarian is pressured into eating meat for the first time, she develops a craving for flesh. Horror, starring Garance Marillier, Ella Rumpf and Rabah Naït Oufella. In French

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King (1975)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine

programme.title: WarGames (1983)
programme.desc: A teenager hacks into a top-secret military supercomputer, which challenges him to what he thinks is a game. However, the machine has complete control of the US nuclear arsenal, and the youngster's actions are unwittingly pushing the world to the brink of war. Thriller, starring Matthew Broderick, Ally Sheedy, Dabney Coleman and John Wood

programme.title: Wonder (2017)
programme.desc: A 10-year-old boy with facial disfigurements that have so far required almost 30 operations takes on a whole new challenge when, after being home educated his entire life, enters mainstream school for the first time. As he struggles to fit in, his fellow pupils and the community are forced to learn the power of compassion and acceptance. Julia Roberts, Owen Wilson and Jacob Tremblay star in Stephen Chbosky's drama, based on the best-selling novel by RJ Palacio

programme.title: Everest (2015)
programme.desc: Two expeditions set out to climb to the summit of the world's highest mountain. Both parties of mountaineers get caught in one of the fiercest snowstorms ever recorded, and face a desperate struggle to survive against the elements. Director Baltasar Kormakur's adventure based on a 1996 expeditition, and co-written by Simon Beaufoy (The Full Monty). The all-star cast includes Jason Clarke, Josh Brolin, Jake Gyllenhaal and Keira Knightley

programme.title: xXx: The Return of Xander Cage (2017)
programme.desc: The extreme sports-loving super spy is brought out of exile when a lethal gang of mercenaries steal a powerful new weapon that threatens the safety of the entire world. He must assemble his own team of adrenaline junkie agents to recover the lethal device. Action thriller, starring Vin Diesel, Donnie Yen, Toni Collette and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: The Gambler (2014)
programme.desc: A literature professor mounts up hundreds of thousands of dollars in debt to a ruthless loan shark and the organiser of an illegal gambling ring after becoming addicted to placing bets. In a last-ditch attempt to pay off his creditors, he risks everything - including his life - on one last game. Thriller, starring Mark Wahlberg, Jessica Lange and John Goodman

programme.title: The Daughter (2015)
programme.desc: A man returning home for his father's wedding uncovers a secret that threatens to destroy the lives of those he left behind. Drama, with Paul Schneider and Geoffrey Rush

programme.title: Stalag 17 (1953)
programme.desc: The inmates of a German PoW camp are convinced there is a traitor in their midst when the guards seem to be forewarned of their escape attempts. An unassuming sergeant content to remain a captive until the end of the conflict becomes the focus of suspicion as mob mentality takes hold. Billy Wilder's Second World War drama, starring an Oscar-winning William Holden, with Don Taylor, Otto Preminger and Harvey Lembeck

programme.title: Mary, Queen of Scots (1971)
programme.desc: Historical biopic about the life and loves of the ill-fated 16th-century monarch, whose opposition to her cousin Elizabeth I led to her downfall and execution. Starring Vanessa Redgrave, Glenda Jackson, Timothy Dalton, Patrick McGoohan, Nigel Davenport and Ian Holm

programme.title: El Dorado (1966)
programme.desc: An ageing gunfighter is drawn into the conflict between a land-grabbing cattle baron and a ranching family, but gets wounded in the process. A year later, he returns to town to find the situation has not improved - so along with his sidekick and the drunken town sheriff, he fights to defend the family from its enemies. Western, starring John Wayne, Robert Mitchum, James Caan and Ed Asner

programme.title: The Monuments Men (2014)
programme.desc: Seven experts in fine art are assembled into a military platoon during the Second World War, and given an unusual mission. They are sent into occupied Europe to recover priceless works of art that have fallen into Nazi hands and ensure the masterpieces survive the conflict. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: Kingsman: The Secret Service (2015)
programme.desc: A streetwise teenager is taken under the wing of his dead father's old colleague, an agent working for a super-secret spy organisation. Will the youngster turn out to be a chip off the old block by proving useful during a battle against a twisted, hi-tech genius? Action comedy based on a comic book, starring Colin Firth, Taron Egerton and Samuel L Jackson

programme.title: Woman at War (2018)
programme.desc: An Icelandic environmental activist single-handedly fights a campaign of illegal sabotage against the aluminium industry. Her priorities shift when her application to adopt a Ukrainian orphan is accepted, leaving her struggling to balance the future of the Earth with the immediate needs of her child. Comedy drama, starring Halldóra Geirharðsdóttir and Jóhann Sigurðarson. In Icelandic, English, Ukrainian, Chinese and Spanish

programme.title: Night Moves (2013)
programme.desc: A farmer, a high-school drop-out and an ex-soldier form a militant environmentalist group, and plot to blow up a controversial dam. While their plan is successful, it has many shocking consequences, and they are faced with a series of moral dilemmas in the aftermath. Thriller, starring Jesse Eisenberg, Dakota Fanning and Peter Sarsgaard

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Ben arranges to meet up with a woman whose phone he has found, however when he ends up running late the father has to send Tucker in his place. Meanwhile, Riley enlists Danny to help her take revenge on an old rival, and Bonnie creates a new business slogan. Comedy series, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau

programme.title: Baby Daddy
programme.desc: Riley makes a bet with Ben that he cannot get a second date with Heather, but when she fails to jump at the chance to go out with him again, Ben resorts to desperate measures to win the wager. Bonnie meets a handsome stranger and considers ending her relationship with Brad. Comedy, starring Jean-Luc Bilodeau and Chelsea Kane

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Groom-to-be Bobby plans a nautical-themed wedding day, but his fiancee Jas dreams of an intimate local wedding

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Love-struck groom Micky has three weeks and £13,000 to plan the perfect wedding for demanding bride-to-be Sophia. However, while Sophia wants a blingy wedding that puts her centre stage, Micky is hoping to impress her with a winter wonderland

programme.title: Made in Chelsea
programme.desc: Love is in the air as Tom Zanetti returns to join Sophie Hermann in Suffolk, while James risks Maeva's wrath when he agrees to one-to-one time with one of the girls in the group

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A no-nonsense Nigerian mum who raises her kids in a strict culture of respect swaps lives with an overwhelmed woman whose disconnected husband and spoilt children run the house

programme.title: Wife Swap USA
programme.desc: A by-the-book school teacher swaps lives with a conspiracy-obsessed home-schooling mum who teaches her kids the world is flat

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef heads to floundering soul food restaurant Blackberry's in Plainfield, New Jersey, but struggles to get his message across to indomitable owner Shelley. Her refusal to deal with the truth comes to a head when Gordon finds a dead mouse in the doorway, prompting him to step up his efforts to change her ways

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. With a menu of more than 126 dishes, the quality of the food is suffering, and even the chefs refuse to eat their own creations

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: First of a two-part special in which Gordon Ramsay heads to the Burger Kitchen in Los Angeles, where tensions are running high because owner Alan Saffron has spent his son's entire inheritance on the struggling restaurant. To make matters worse, the kitchen is run by a chef called David Blaine who shares nothing of his famous namesake's magic when it comes to cooking - and whose threatening behaviour regularly gets him into trouble

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny gets more than she bargained for when Leonard agrees to meet a psychiatrist on her behalf, while Sheldon and Raj collaborate on an astronomical discovery. Stuart moves out, but Howard and Bernadette's happiness is short-lived. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After being forced to deal with a sick Sheldon, the rest of the gang try to treat themselves to a Sheldon-free weekend. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's grandmother, known as Meemaw, arrives in Pasadena to visit, but his excitement at her arrival quickly gives way to discomfort when she clashes with Amy. Meanwhile, Raj finds himself drawn to an ambitious female screenwriter he meets at the comic-book store, but Bernadette angrily reminds him he already has a girlfriend. Comedy, guest starring June Squibb

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Nurse Christine battles to clear her airway, while Maureen's husband Preston waits in the relatives' room. As doctors carry out further investigations, Preston joins Maureen and it's revealed how their world was turned upside down when Maureen was diagnosed with the neurological condition multiple system atrophy. Ten-year-old Ryan is brought into resus having fallen at school and suffered internal bleeding

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark, a 35-year-old long-term resident at a neurological hospital, is rushed to St George's struggling to breathe, and his devoted parents are with him as doctors attempt to get to the bottom of his symptoms. Three-year-old Thomas is brought in with a high temperature by his parents, who are concerned as he was born with an extremely rare blood condition that meant he needed a full blood exchange when he was less than 24 hours old

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Cheating in the football match backfires on Ted, prompting his Rugged Island rivals to think up a devilish forfeit - and leaving the priest trying to contrive a way of kicking Bishop Brennan's backside and getting away with it. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to Chile to look into coloured crisps and find out how these rainbow snacks get their colour. Jimmy Doherty travels to Spain to investigate one of the trendiest items on the shelves, avocados, and the rapidly rising demand for these fruits which is leading to alarming reports of a crime wave hitting growers

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a Cornish couple working to restore a luxury American trailer, and a former taxi driver who is building a writing den in her garden on a budget of just £800, using only recycled materials retrieved from the local dump. Plus, George's Spanish tour continues in Andalusia and a luxury mobile home provides inspiration for the big build

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Sue and Simon bought their four-bedroom Hertfordshire home in 2014, but the fake-Victorian extension has given the property a bad layout which is proving something of a nightmare. The couple also believe their home is by far the ugliest on their street. Enter architect Laura Jane Clark, who specialises in breathing new life into unappealing spaces. She has a plan to entirely transform the interior by moving the staircase, putting in skylights and installing an innovative revolving wall. However, Sue and Simon have a tight budget of £50,000 - and that's before they've even started on the exterior

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits a Grade-II listed worker's cottage in Hampshire, as his mission to future-proof historic houses continues. Due to restrictions on planning permissions, the property has become somewhat disorganised, and is in need of a careful makeover. Seeking inspiration, George tours a Victorian stable that was renovated to add a gym, cinema and roof garden, and heads to a farmhouse in East Sussex to view a smart and stylish solution to the problem of designing new, accessible bathrooms for period properties

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Golf House on the Isle of Wight, where hosts John and Sue Blakemore hope to impress their guests during their stay. Things start off positively with a well-received breakfast, but a suspicious stain in a toilet causes controversy among some of the competitors

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to St John's Wood, north-west London, where Nick Tyrie and his friend Layla Amini welcome their guests to the New Inn Pub. One of the proprietors tries to win points by revealing her musical past, but John and Sue Blakemore feel their room is too impersonal, and are underwhelmed by the choices at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the Bunkroom hostel in Chester, David Sharrock is determined to prove that hostelling is for everybody - even if his wife Victoria isn't convinced. It's bunkbeds and budget breakfasts for all, as his fellow hosts decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group heads to John Graham's the B&B in Blackpool for the final visit of the week, and an afternoon of tenpin bowling brings out the guests' competitive sides. Later, there is more tension at the dinner table when the host is subjected to aggressive questioning by John and Sue Blakemore

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the guest house owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service that they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winner is announced

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Belinda travels to Dick and Angel's chateau so that Angel can teach her the art of decoupage on a classic 1950s family dressing table. It's changeover day at Krys and Tim's chateau, as they sweat it out in 42-degree heat to get their 20-room chateau ready for their next booking. Budding chateau owners Elaine and David visit a 19th-century chateau with 18 rooms and two gîtes in Normandy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick and Angel invite Tim and Krys over to their place as Dick shows Tim how to put on a gourmet weekend, and Angel has a heart-to-heart chat about chateau-living with Krys. Lee wrestles with a curtain in the shower room on arrival day for his and Belinda's first paying guests. Plus, Angela and Steve invite eight friends over to test-run their tandem holiday business

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Ye Olde Fighting Cocks in Arnside, Cumbria, where owners Tony Keates and Jeanette Hamer hope to prove that their establishment offers the best value for money. However, while the B&B's guests are bowled over by the spectacular views, they are less than impressed by Tony's tales of business success and a mouldy mattress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Hadleigh, Suffolk, where Dr Stephen Stanley-Little invites fellow B&B owners to come and stay at his tranquil country setting - Westward Cottage. He takes his guests on a historic village tour that leaves one couple plotting their escape, while the host's relaxed approach to breakfast drives some of the others mad

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Third to host are bohemian couple Avril Berry and Alan Fraser of Roulotte Retreat in Melrose in the Scottish Borders. Their unique gypsy caravans are not to everyone's liking, and a yoga class fails to calm the tensions that seem to be rising within the group

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Elgin Hotel, Blackpool, where manager Garry Lloyd and his daughter Sophie Seddon want to demonstrate how their 89-room business can still deliver a personal touch. The hosts arrange an afternoon of tap-dancing lessons, ending with a surprise performance at the iconic Grand Theatre, but a breakfast mix-up the following morning puts some of the guests nonplussed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants featured throughout the week reunite to revisit the highs and lows of their various stays, and as the B&B owners confront their critics face to face, they discover what the rival parties really thought of the quality of service they encountered. After the deliberations are over, the winning guest house is announced

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Birmingham try to stop hypothermia from setting in when a woman collapses in the snow. At the same time, Mark and Adi attend to a woman whose waters may have broken at 27 weeks and could be hours away from giving birth. In Coventry, crewmates Neil and Laura treat an elderly man who has a life-threatening infection

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient is brought in having broken his leg playing American football, while a resident at a neurological hospital is examined for signs that he has suffered a stroke. A man experiencing dizzy spells is accompanied on a trip to St George's by his granddaughter, who shares her memories of how he filled a gap in her life after her father died

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Exploring the hopes and expectations of three different men and how they respond to changes that are beyond their control. They include Stanlie, 24, who is airlifted to St George's by helicopter paramedics after being impaled on a metal rod at a construction site

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics fly to the aid of mountain biker Rob in the Cumbrian mountains. Off-road motorbiker Dean is set to miss his friend's 50th birthday celebrations after taking a nasty tumble. Plus, 15-year-old Robbie needs urgent care when he falls from a coastal cliff in South Shields

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Birmingham try to stop hypothermia from setting in when a woman collapses in the snow. At the same time, Mark and Adi attend to a woman whose waters may have broken at 27 weeks and could be hours away from giving birth. In Coventry, crewmates Neil and Laura treat an elderly man who has a life-threatening infection

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A patient is brought in having broken his leg playing American football, while a resident at a neurological hospital is examined for signs that he has suffered a stroke. A man experiencing dizzy spells is accompanied on a trip to St George's by his granddaughter, who shares her memories of how he filled a gap in her life after her father died

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Exploring the hopes and expectations of three different men and how they respond to changes that are beyond their control. They include Stanlie, 24, who is airlifted to St George's by helicopter paramedics after being impaled on a metal rod at a construction site

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Mrs Doyle wins a competition to meet a famous singer, and discreetly invites him round for tea - but the secret slips out, and soon the house is besieged by hordes of adoring pensioners. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Pauline McLynn

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Devon County Show, where she discovers the secret of home-made handscrub and learns about beekeeping and making beeswax candles. She also tries her hand at crafting jewellery in silver clay - an ideal way of making unusual but inexpensive gifts

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Dave and Debbie Chambers from Redditch, Worcestershire, started their family young, but now their four children have grown up, they've decided to recapture their youth and buy a place in Spain where they can enjoy time in the sun - just the two of them. For their budget of £45,000 Laura Hamilton shows them five apartments in and around the town of Torrevieja, but will any of them be their perfect place?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien hope a clay pigeon trap, a searchlight and a Peugeot 205 will help make a profit for their owners

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A simple case becomes complicated when family secrets are uncovered, while another reveals a great-grandfather's life on the docks in the early days of diving. An uncle's part in the defeat of Nazi Germany in the Second World War is also highlighted. Plus, details of unclaimed estates where heirs still need to be traced

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: A straight-talking hairdresser from London tries to cut it as Costa Blanca's newest stylist and newlyweds start a new life as holiday hosts in the south of France

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Unicorn Inn in Ludlow, Shropshire, where husband and wife team Lee and Zoe Osbourne hope their glamorous fairy-tale style rooms will impress

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the East Walls Hotel in Chichester, West Sussex, owned by history-loving husband and wife Celia and Damon Haughey, who have rejected the boutique label

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at The Wheatsheaf in Sandbach, Cheshire, where owner Jake Pare and general manager Chris Baldwin hope to impress their guests

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay of the week is at the Shawries Hotel in Blackpool, where the dated decor takes two guests back to their childhood and breakfast is a build-your-own sandwich platter

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. East Walls Hotel is first up, and owners Celia and Damon need to clean up Dave's toilet brush troubles before broaching his beef with the breakfast banger

programme.title: Grand Designs New Zealand
programme.desc: The daughter of a world-famous motorbike inventor takes on the multi-million-dollar task of restoring the eclectic earthquake-damaged family home in Christchurch

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Dagmar the giraffe is supporting her closest herd member Orla, whose new calf is overdue - but at the same time she is reluctant to allow her own youngest to feed properly. So the keepers plan a herd birth in the hope that both mothers can learn from each other. There is concern when two of the spider monkeys withdraw from their family, while two devoted camels experience the trauma of separation when one undergoes an operation

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Donna and Paul move into their new home in western France, but struggle with the language, while Adam from Warwickshire is hoping to join the band of expat chateau owners

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud returns to north Cornwall to see one of his favourite Grand Designs, and discover whether Rebecca Sturrock and Gregory Kewish were successful with their ambitious plans. Their project, to reinforce the walls of an old bungalow and put a new living space on top made entirely of cross-laminated timber, soon ran into difficulties, but now fully complete both inside and out, Kevin finds out whether this innovative house is really big enough to accommodate a growing family

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: A visual journey around Snowdonia, starting at the coastal town of Harlech and taking in the Italianate village of Portmeirion, Bala Lake, and the slate mine of Dinorwic

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Featuring people who have faced challenges and transformed their lives, including Nicole, who burnt her foot with caustic soda while cleaning her drains. Nicole reveals that she was born a man, and while growing up in Scarborough, she dreamed of becoming a pop star. Cameras also focus on Francis, 28, who has fallen off his bike and is suffering from severe amnesia

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy words-and-numbers quiz, as Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams compete against Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson. David Baddiel joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Britain's Most Beautiful Landscapes
programme.desc: A visual journey around Snowdonia, starting at the coastal town of Harlech and taking in the Italianate village of Portmeirion, Bala Lake, and the slate mine of Dinorwic

programme.title: Extraordinary Escapes with Sandi Toksvig
programme.desc: The presenter explores the UK's wildest, most remote and beautifully designed holiday homes, accompanied by some of the nation's most fascinating and funny women. This time, Sandi is joined by the comedian, broadcaster and her friend Sindhu Vee to explore some of the best staycations Scotland has to offer. First, the pair visit a private island off the coast of Skye, before checking into a luxury shooting lodge in the Highlands. Finally, they arrive at a truly unique, elliptical shaped home built to celebrate the island views of the Inner Hebrides and the Sound of Mull

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty heads on the trail of an elusive fruit - the fresh date, visiting a vast water purification plant in Israel where the raw sewage of Tel Aviv is harnessed to turn barren desert into fertile farmland. Kate Quilton flies to California to investigate why almond butter is more expensive than other nutty spreads, where she discovers a combination of extreme weather and global economics have affected the price of almonds. Matt Tebbutt makes a surprising discovery about the true origins of haggis

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Series following Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald who earn a living by restoring forgotten relics to their former glory

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef samples Mediterranean food and visits his favourite places in the region. In the first programme he catches a ferry to the mountainous island of Corsica, where he tastes sausages and sheep's cheese, and discovers the locals have a passion for chestnuts

programme.title: Rick Stein's Mediterranean Escapes
programme.desc: The chef continues to explore his favourite locations in the Mediterranean, visiting a lagoon in Sardinia where he watches fishermen catching mullet using ancient Roman methods. He also boards a ferry heading for the city of Palermo in Sicily and discovers one of the most impressive pasta dishes of his career

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Former boxer Brandon Bernier and his wife Lori are anxious to see what is inside a locked coffin, but a rival thwarts their plans

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: The competing bidders aim to win two speciality items at auction, including a six-ton trailer. Reality series following a group of rival lock-up enthusiasts

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Engineer Ralph Hosier and his two teams of car buffs build scrap cars to try in a bid to out-brake Shane Lynch's Jaguar F-Type SVR. Can their imagination and never-say-die attitude overcome millions of pounds of automotive design?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a project to build a car that can turn into a hovercraft and bring relief to flooded areas. Their efforts do not start smoothly, but it is not long before the trio are demonstrating the brilliance of their creation with an eventful journey down the River Avon. Plus, Jeremy grabs the keys to two variants of the Mercedes-Benz SLS AMG supercar - the V8-engined Black Series with a roaring 622bhp, and the 740bhp Electric Drive, which is powered by four electric motors. X-Men and Les Miserables star Hugh Jackman is the latest celebrity to take the new Reasonably Priced Car around the test track

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race
programme.desc: The crowds gather in Switzerland for more downhill drama, applauding the ingenuity of these amateur teams as the racers crash into hay bales

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May take a look at increasingly popular tall hatchbacks known as crossovers. Having concluded their primary appeal is to caravanners, the duo spend a couple of days in models by Mazda and Volkswagen and try to get a feel for what life is like for the people they believe would buy one. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond drives a pair of exciting Lamborghinis in Italy - the ferocious Aventador Roadster and the limited-edition Sesto Elemento, which carries an eye-watering price tag of around £2million. Two cars are also put through their paces on the test track, with James in a modern re-creation of the classic Porsche 911 and Aerosmith frontman Steven Tyler in the rather less exotic Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson drives the Jaguar F-Type sports car on some of Britain's finest roads, while Richard Hammond jumps inside the Range Rover Sport and gives it a thorough workout off-road, on a track and in what he believes is its most natural habitat - the lanes of Cheshire. James May goes for a countryside adventure in the New Routemaster London bus brought into use in 2013, which is propelled by a turbodiesel generator and a powerful electric motor. Inspired by the machines they have tested, the trio then meet to celebrate Britain's motor industry with a grand finale in front of Buckingham Palace. Plus, former Formula One driver Mark Webber takes to the track for a lap in the Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Fast Justice
programme.desc: The Sentinels find a concealed blade in a passenger's trousers. Elsewhere, a trio of cars linked to drug dealing play a game of cat and mouse with the cops

programme.title: Border Force: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Officers are on alert for a cartel ambush as they transfer a Sicario to the Mexican military. A random inspection of a pick-up truck reveals 32kg of narcotics

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The presenters and the Stig enter a 24-hour endurance race at Silverstone, which proves to be one of their toughest challenges to date. Plus, James May races against BMX bikers around Budapest in a Fiat 500 and Jeremy Clarkson test-drives the Ascari A10. Actor Keith Allen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: The newlyweds return from their honeymoon to a big welcome from both their families. However, Smithy seems disconcerted by his pal's return and Nessa makes a confession that spoils the happy occasion. Comedy, starring Mathew Horne, James Corden and Joanna Page

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy goes missing following Nessa's shocking revelation, and the boys try to track him down to persuade him to accept his responsibilities. Meanwhile, Stacey is forced to contemplate the consequences of transforming her long-distance relationship with Gavin into a functioning marriage

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: Smithy accompanies Nessa to the hospital for her first ultrasound scan, and the pair catch a glimpse of their baby. Stacey becomes unusually excited at the prospect of a weekend visit to her family home after she struggles to adjust to her role as an 'Essex wife' - which proves to lack some of the glamour she had expected

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as James Acaster, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Darren Harriott and Zoe Lyons join regular panellist Hugh Dennis to take a comic look at the news. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, London Hughes, Rhys James and Milton Jones join regular panellist Hugh Dennis on the comedy quiz show, hosted by Dara O Briain. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on each other

programme.title: Insert Name Here
programme.desc: Comedians Nish Kumar and Katherine Ryan, historian Kate Williams and sports presenter Gabby Logan join host Sue Perkins and captains Richard Osman and Josh Widdicombe as the comedy quiz returns. The two teams answer questions about famous people who share a name, with the winning trio getting to decide the greatest-ever bearer of this week's moniker

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Extended edition in which panellists Jo Brand, Bill Bailey, James Acaster and Alan Davies compete, as Stephen Fry mixes and matches a melange of M-subjects. As usual, the host poses questions that are so difficult, points are awarded for the answers he finds most interesting

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Jack Dee hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by observational comedian Seann Walsh and king of the one-liners Milton Jones

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Match of the Day presenter Gary Lineker takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Tom Allen and political commentator Ayesha Hazarika joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier
programme.desc: Jon and guests Richard Osman, Jessie Cave and Darren Harriott discuss, categorise and rank worries to do with Modern Living. Has inventing got out of hand? Is magic dead?

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Stand-up and actress Jo Brand takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster and journalist Grace Dent and comedian Kiri Pritchard-McLean joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Late Nite Eats
programme.desc: Chef Jordan Andino is on the hunt to find restaurants that reign supreme in the late-night food scene, meeting other chefs to taste their most popular dishes

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Frank falls ill, so Mike's brother Robbie takes his place on the latest expedition, which leads to a set of antique spurs that could be worth a fortune

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies sets tasks for Roisin Conaty, Tim Key, Romesh Ranganathan, Frank Skinner and Josh Widdicombe. They have to identify the contents of a table full of mystery pies, make a film backwards and high five a 55 year old. programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart

programme.title: Living With Hitler
programme.desc: The events that the German population and the people of the occupied countries lived through in the Nazi era from 1933 to 1945. The opening episode focuses on how Hitler's grip on power becomes absolute, as the Nazi flag flew everywhere and the Nazi salute became compulsory

programme.title: Living With Hitler
programme.desc: We look at the racially unified People's Community, the diplomatic successes of the Third Reich, growing anti-Semitism across Germany, and the lead up to WWII

programme.title: Living With Hitler
programme.desc: Life on the Homefront and abroad is examined: from rationing and bereavement, the Allied bombing campaign, the Holocaust and finally, the fall of the Third Reich

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970 Excalibur Roadstar, once owned by Roy Orbison, arrives at Thornton-le-Dale and Derek heads to County Durham to collect a 1935 Austin 7 Ulster

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Two iconic classics find their way to the Mathewsons' auction room. Dave heads to north Manchester to pick up an Austin A30 and a Morris 8E that's had an alternative paint job

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: An impressive collection of five iconic cars arrives at Thornton le Dale, including a 1967 Crayford Corsair and a 1966 Ford Zodiac which emerge from a garage in Selby

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough continues his exploration of the continent in east Africa, which is a haven for life and supports a higher density of large mammals than anywhere else on Earth. From dense forests to snow-capped peaks, steamy swamps to endless savannah, it's also a land of unpredictable extremes, where creatures must adapt to survive. In Tanzania, a tiny lizard risks its life to eat flies from the face of a sleepy lion. The nesting habits of the 4ft shoebill are also captured on camera - the giant bird has two chicks, but due to sibling rivalry only one will survive. On the plains of Amboseli, a baby elephant is near to death in the drought, so its mother leaves the herd to stay with her offspring until the bitter end

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the varied creatures of the Congo rainforest, one of the most competitive habitats on Earth. A sweet-toothed chimpanzee uses a complex array of tools to access honey from a beehive, the elusive picathartes bird feeds unsuspecting frogs to its dinosaur-like chicks and a massive female python reveals her maternal side as she sunbathes to incubate her eggs. Tiny leaf-folding frogs battle it out kung fu style, and as darkness falls the forest elephants gather to face their rivals

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough describes some of the wildlife spectacles of southern Africa, starting out in a little-explored rainforest discovered less than a decade ago by scientists who spotted it on satellite maps. There, thousands of butterflies dance for the right to mate, while on a tiny island off Mozambique, green turtles emerge from eggs and begin a hazardous journey to the water - with pied crows and hungry crabs eager to get them. Cameras also capture one of the largest gatherings of great white sharks on the planet as 30 of the predators circle around a dead whale

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the wildlife eking out an existence in and around the scorching wilderness of the Sahara, where only the toughest survive. It's a constant struggle against dehydration, as zebra battle over dwindling rivers, camels search for water and tiny barn swallows navigate thousands of square miles of barren sand to find a life-saving oasis. The naked mole rat avoids the heat by living a subterranean existence, while the silver ant is able to take on the midday sun - but needs a 'spacesuit' to do it

programme.title: Africa
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the vast and diverse continent, aiming to show its landscapes and wildlife as they have never been seen before. He begins in the Kalahari desert, Africa's south-west corner, where clever meerkats are outsmarted by a wily bird's use of mimicry, a solitary rhino is seen to exhibit previously unknown sociable behaviours, terrifying insects prey on baby birds and the rarest fish in the world is filmed for the first time. In the neighbouring Namib desert, which receives almost no rain, the competition for scant resources can be fierce, as proven by a ferocious fight for supremacy between two giraffes

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor tempts fate by agreeing to look after neighbours Patrick and Pippa's house while they're away - a situation made even more volatile by the arrival of his brother Alfred, who proceeds to rub him up the wrong way. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson, Annette Crosbie and Richard Pearson

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household falls foul of an evil curse, so Victor resolves to remove it. However, it soon becomes clear the task is easier said than done. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of 1970s-era axe-men, acoustic virtuosos and thumping riff merchants, in a compilation of guitar-heavy performances from the BBC TV archives. Guitar gods including Jimi Hendrix, Pete Townshend, Peter Green and Johnny Winter are joined by, among others, flamenco maestro Manitas De Plata, bottleneck bluesman Ry Cooder and straight-up rockers AC/DC and Thin Lizzy. Plus, Fleetwood Mac, the Jam and Be Bop Deluxe and Nils Lofgren

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Status Quo play Pictures of Matchstick Men on Top of the Pops in 1968, the Who perform Long Live Rock in the Old Grey Whistle Test studio, Dire Straits deliver Tunnel of Love and Lynyrd Skynyrd bring a taste of the American South with Sweet Home Alabama. The programme also features rare performances from George Benson, Leo Kottke, Link Wray and Tom Petty

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. Underwater time-lapse photography also shows how carnivorous corals feed at night and how, over a few nights of the year, they disperse their sperm and eggs

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The effects of the region's powerful hurricanes on the islands' wildlife and the survival stories of some of the species, including how the Puerto Rican parrot almost became extinct. Offshore, lobsters head to deeper waters for refuge, while reefs benefit from the destruction

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The region's mysterious side comes to light during a journey from the rainforests of Panama to the Barrier Reef of the Americas - the world's second largest coral system. The programme also explores the wildlife on the shores of Central America, including killer whales and thimble jellyfish in the deep waters near Honduras

programme.title: Wild Japan

programme.title: Wild Japan

programme.title: Wild Japan

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: The Colonel and Gruber's escape plan has gone awry, so they try to make general Von Klinkerhoffen believe the Resistance had kidnapped them

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave finds a very rare 70s Sunbeam Lotus that has been rotting away in a farmyard for 29 years, while a Morris Minor convertible goes under the hammer

programme.desc: Part one of two. A re-examination of Jack the Ripper's crimes, revealing how the story known today was shaped by the sensationalist press of 1888. This documentary strips back decades of rumour and misinformation to reveal the true lives of the five women slain

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates what Hitler intended to do with the priceless works of art stolen by the Nazis from across Europe, as well as his obsession with religious artefacts

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The largest and most sophisticated radio telescope on the planet, which draws inspiration from geodesic dome designs and the work of 18th-century engineer Jean Marie Jacquard

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of London's Crossrail network, a project of staggering proportions, with 10,000 personnel working across 40 sites and on 118km of track. The focus should have been on the impressive engineering feat, though delays and budgets have become the focus of attention

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo journeys from Glasgow to Cumbrae, learning about a forgotten Scottish expedition to the Antarctic and how one woman led a protest against high rents in the city

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo falls into line with the University of Glasgow's Officer Training Corps, where he joins students for drill. The political pundit then heads west along the Firth of Clyde to Helensburgh, where he discovers a pioneering group of artists known as the Glasgow Boys. The idyllic West Highland Line takes Michael deep into the Highlands to Inveraray and the ancestral home of the Clan Campbell and Dukes of Argyll. Michael's final stop is Connel Ferry, near Oban, where, on the Achnacloich Estate, he discovers a Highland calf whose breed has become an icon of the Highlands

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Helmingham Hall in Suffolk, where items include a sapphire ring and a sketch by Robert Baden-Powell, founder of the Boy Scouts and Girl Guides. Glass expert Andy McConnell challenges the owner of a collection of mid-18th century drinking glasses to identify the lone fake, while an intricate model of a butcher's shop intrigues Fergus Gambon

programme.title: The Railway: First Great Western
programme.desc: Bruce Parkin steps off a train with his family to attend his retirement party after 52 years' service, making him one of Europe's longest-serving drivers. The redevelopment of Reading station is the largest overhaul on the Great Western network since the days of Brunel, and Murray Parkin and his team carry out the last engine change at the historic Cow Lane site before they head over to their new state-of-the-art terminal

programme.title: Frozen Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough travels to the Arctic and Antarctic to explore the wildernesses at Earth's poles, investigating the wildlife and geographical features found there. He begins by travelling to the North Pole, where two polar bears reveal a surprisingly tender side to their characters, before heading to the South Pole - 100 years after Roald Amundsen and Robert Falcon Scott first reached it

programme.title: Madagascar
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates the story of the island in the Indian Ocean, off the southeastern coast of Africa. It has an ecosystem that has stood in isolation for millions of years, and produced an array of wildlife that cannot be found anywhere else on the planet. He begins by observing lemurs in their habitat, male red giraffe-necked weevils fighting each other, the courting rituals of chameleons, and the behaviour of spiders and fossas

programme.title: The Buildings That Fought Hitler
programme.desc: Numerous pill boxes, gun emplacements and dragon's teeth were all tactically positioned to slow the Germans trying to get to the major cities

programme.title: True Evil: The Making of a Nazi
programme.desc: A profile of Hermann Goering - the dashing, young, down-on-his-luck war veteran who turned into an extravagant, heartless, obese, drug-addled Nazi billionaire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

programme.title: WW2 - Frontlines
programme.desc: Midway became the location for the most decisive naval battle in the Pacific War. The Japanese should have triumphed, yet the Americans turned the Japanese trap into a bold ambush

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Guided missiles, the Wasserfall, and the war-changing V2 were dreamed up and tested in Peenemunde, a German seaside city suddenly overtaken by Nazi elite

programme.title: Nazi Hunters
programme.desc: The people responsible for tracking down war criminals who had evaded capture, beginning with the US army task force in charge of locating German scientists

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: An advanced German battleship that sank in a Norwegian fjord during the Second World War, the Miles Glacier Bridge in Alaska, and a mysterious concrete giant in Bulgaria

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.desc: Salesman Mike Brewer and mechanic Ant Anstead take on a 1987 Alfa Romeo Spider Quadrifoglio with a rare hard-top. The car needs extensive repairs, including a rebuild of the propshaft

programme.desc: The team takes an impressive custom car, a 1956 Chrysler 300B Custom hardtop, to the Barrett-Jackson Scottsdale auction

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

In the south of France, Tim and Sasha set out to turn their chateau's original bathing pool - which has sat empty for 15 years - into a unique feature for a new wellness garden. Plus, Fiona unveils a plaque and hosts a party to celebrate her chateau's 845th birthday. To ensure the drinks are served at the right temperature, she transforms one of the chateau's old dungeons into the perfect wine cellar

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: David and Megan Penny from Gloucestershire search for a holiday home in the Axarquia area of southern Spain, where they can enjoy a laid-back lifestyle with their dog. Property expert Laura Hamilton helps them in their endeavours

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Stephen and Janis Pigeon have a budget of £150,000 to move to the western Costa del Sol. Having sold their Buckinghamshire home, the pressure is on for Jasmine Harman to find them a suitable place in the Spanish region reasonably quickly

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton meets hospital worker Sharon, who is searching for a holiday home in Torrevieja on Spain's Costa Blanca with a budget of £50,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

They have looked at 300 properties online but only viewed three in person

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets Ian and Becky from Lincolnshire, who are embarking on the restoration of a rare VW Phoenix campervan. But this couple are not doing things by halves, having decided to add a sound-activated dance floor to the vehicle. He also visits Stirlingshire, where a small family business plans to convert a Sea King helicopter into luxury overnight accommodation, and travels to an ordinary residential street on the outskirts of Auckland, New Zealand, to see a dome home that is out of this world. Plus, George and Will design a front door for their rotating house

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Emilia Fox and Professor David Wilson, one of Britain's leading criminologists, investigate famous unsolved murder cases. Thirteen-year-old David Spencer and 11-year-old Patrick Warren went missing in Birmingham on Boxing Day in 1996. To this day, no one has been charged with the double disappearance and murder of the two friends, and their bodies have never been found despite two major police investigations and a missing persons campaign that featured the boys' pictures on milk cartons. But now, Emilia and David discover new evidence leading to a prime suspect

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: An on-call stroke doctor has a busy shift in the resus department, and shares his personal experiences of working on the front line during the first week of the pandemic. When a 19-year-old is treated for injuries sustained in a motorbike crash, his mother reflects on leaving her children in Colombia in search of a better life for them abroad. Other patients include a builder with an ankle injury sustained playing basketball, and a 72-year-old with a suspected stroke

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: A rolling cast of famous faces critiques shows including The Masked Dancer, Dirty Dancing, Best of Top Gear, Naked Attraction, Tom Allen's Quizness, Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back, and The Bad Skin Clinic. programme.title: Naked Attraction

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
